  • Build and maintain data components (event-driven ingestion, transformations, APIs, catalogs) using modern data tools.
  • Write SQL-first transformations (dbt layers + normalization framework) and Python where orchestration/API logic needs it.
  • Implement robust data quality as early as possible in the pipeline (shift-left approach) for validation, monitoring, and alerting systems at scale.
  • Optimize data models for performance, cost and reliability across multiple datasets.
  • Collaborate with Junior/Intern team members on Data craft best practices and tooling.

Who We’re Looking For

  • Data Engineers / Data Analysts with 2 to 4 years of experience who excel at building reliable, scalable data models and pipelines that power AI decision-making at enterprise scale.
  • Hands-on builders with:
    • Strong SQL (complex joins, aggregations, incremental patterns)
    • Python for services/pipelines (not notebook-only analytics)
    • Comfort with git, PR reviews, pytest
    • Interest in business domain (not just infra)
  • Strong collaborators who partner with Data Practitioners, Data Scientists, Software/ML Engineers, Product teams, and business stakeholders to turn raw data into actionable customer insights.
  • Startup mindset: thrives in ambiguity, proactively solves complex data challenges, and improves tooling beyond your immediate scope.
  • Ready to Lead Boldly – building the data foundation that enables Aily's AI platform to deliver millions of real-time insights, briefings and agentic responses daily.
Location & details

  • Locations: Madrid (MAD), Barcelona (BCN) and Munich (MUC).
  • Contract type: Permanent.
  • Work policy: hybrid (2 days per week in the office).

About Us

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Descripción de la empresa

MSX International es un líder global en servicios para el sector de automoción, presente en más de 80 países. Desde nuestra sede en Barcelona trabajamos con diferentes fabricantes internacionales para ofrecer soluciones innovadoras que impulsan la eficiencia, la calidad y el rendimiento de sus operaciones.

Si buscas un entorno dinámico, analítico, internacional y orientado a la mejora continua, este es tu sitio.

Descripción del empleo

Como Analista de Datos, serás responsable de gestionar, analizar y transformar datos clave relacionados con proyectos del sector automoción. Tu misión será convertir grandes volúmenes de datos en insights claros y accionables que apoyen la toma de decisiones, la optimización de procesos y el rendimiento general del negocio.

Requisitos


Realizar análisis diarios, semanales y mensuales utilizando herramientas como Tableau, Power BI y Excel
Identificar tendencias, variaciones y oportunidades de mejora
Preparar informes avanzados alineados con KPIs y requisitos del proyecto
Mantener, actualizar y asegurar la calidad de la documentación y reportes en herramientas como SharePoint y Office365
Identificar patrones en los datos y proponer acciones correctivas u oportunidades de optimización
Colaborar con equipos multifuncionales para convertir insights en acciones concretas
Garantizar el cumplimiento de estándares internos en informes y dashboards
Presentar análisis, conclusiones e información relevante a equipos internos y dirección
Asegurar la precisión, consistencia y calidad de los datos analizados
Proponer mejoras continuas en procesos, metodologías y flujos de análisis


Requisitos

Conocimientos y habilidades


Habilidades de comunicación y capacidad para trabajar con equipos diversos
Enfoque resolutivo para problemas complejos
Capacidad para gestionar prioridades y trabajar con plazos ajustados
Dominio de Tableau, Power BI y Excel
Conocimientos de sistemas de gestión de datos, minería de datos, procesos ETL y reporting automatizado
Competencias en análisis estadístico y modelos de rendimiento
Experiencia con herramientas de automatización (RPA) es un plus
Conocimiento de Microsoft Dynamics (deseable)


Formación


Grado en Ingeniería, Economía, Estadística, Matemáticas o formación equivalente


Experiencia


Experiencia previa demostrable como Analista de Datos


Idiomas


Español nativo
Inglés B2 (escrito y hablado)


Información adicional

Qué ofrecemos


Puesto permanente con contrato indefinido desde el primer día.
Política de teletrabajo de hasta el 50% de la jornada mensual tras el periodo de adaptación.
Formar parte de un gran equipo de profesionales en una compañía internacional consolidada.
Obtener una visión global del sector automoción, interactuando con clientes, proveedores, consultores y concesionarios.
Salario: 23.000 euros brutos anuales.
Acceso a MSX Bazaar, el portal de compras colectivas con grandes descuentos para empleados.
Posibilidad de adherirte al Plan de Retribución Flexible (seguro médico, tarjeta transporte, tarjeta restaurante, formación...).
23 días laborables de vacaciones al año según convenio y 3 días por año completo trabajado.
Incentivos adicionales por programas de recomendación interna, formadores internos, detección de nuevas oportunidades de negocio, etc.

Title: Business Intelligence Data Analyst
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
En BETWEEN apostamos por ofrecerte las mejores oportunidades dentro del área tecnológica. Contamos con un equipo de más de 1000 profesionales.

Si quieres desarrollar tus capacidades en un entorno internacional, apostando por trabajar en soluciones de I+D tanto en Ingeniería como en IT, ¡este es tu próximo destino!

Damos soporte en una gran variedad de áreas como Operaciones, Desarrollo de producto, Compras, Firmware, Software, Business Intelligence, Gestión, Transformación digital, ¡y muchas más! Además, desarrollamos soluciones tecnológicas a medida desde nuestros Hubs Tecnológicos.

La persona seleccionada formará parte de un equipo multidisciplinar, donde se valora especialmente la calidad, la innovación y la excelencia técnica, en un entorno de I+D orientado a la mejora continua y al desarrollo de soluciones de alto impacto para el negocio.

¿Qué haré? Estas serán tus funciones principales:


Diseñar, desarrollar y mantener dashboards avanzados en Power BI, asegurando altos estándares de calidad, rendimiento y usabilidad.
Analizar y resolver incidencias en los dashboards existentes, garantizando su estabilidad y fiabilidad.
Realizar el modelado de datos, optimizando estructuras y flujos de información.
Desarrollar y optimizar consultas SQL para la extracción, transformación y análisis de datos.
Colaborar en un entorno internacional, utilizando inglés oral de forma habitual en el día a día.


¿Cómo lo haré?


Tendrás contrato indefinido y horario flexible
Podrás trabajar en modelo híbrido. Trabajarás 3 días a la semana en la oficina de Sant Cugat del Vallès. El resto de días puedes teletrabajar.
Tendrás un PC y los periféricos necesarios para el desarrollo de tu trabajo.
Trabajarás en un entorno internacional.


Si Eres BETWEENER Obtendrás Estos Beneficios

Ofrecemos un paquete completo de beneficios que te permitirá desenvolverte tanto profesional como personalmente. Algunos de ellos son:


Seguro médico con Alan Seguros.
Oportunidades de formación: Idiomas, Certificaciones, Bootcamps y mucho más.
Multitud de eventos: BETWEEN Night, Torneo de Padel y mucho más.
Retribución flexible (restaurant, transporte, guardería).
Incentivos por validar o recomendar talento.


Requisitos

Requisitos imprescindibles


Grado en ADE, Ingeniería o titulaciones afines.
Experiencia mínima de 2 años trabajando con Power BI (modelado de datos, DAX, Power Query, etc.).
Sólidos conocimientos en SQL.
Inglés avanzado (C1), con capacidad para comunicarse fluidamente en entornos profesionales.


Requisitos valorables


Experiencia en entornos de I+D o equipos multidisciplinares.
Experiencia en procesos de ETL / ELT y orquestación de datos (por ejemplo, Azure Data Factory, Airflow u otros).
Conocimientos en lenguajes backend (Python) o plataformas de datos (Databricks).
Capacidad analítica y enfoque a negocio.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
The Role

As a Data Analyst, you’ll help our customers get up and running on Affinity by importing their data and setting up the platform to support their workflows. You’ll work closely with our Onboarding, Implementation, and Customer Success teams, and play a key role in communicating directly with customers to gather requirements, set expectations, and deliver clear, actionable reports that demonstrate the value of their data setup.

What you’ll do:


Import and transform customer CSV data into Affinity and configure tables, lists, and fields so that customers can hit the ground running.
Manage the data import queue with discipline — tracking status, ETA, priority, and next steps for every import.
Clean, deduplicate, and validate data to ensure high-quality imports.
Work with Onboarding and Implementation teams to map customer data to Affinity’s data model.
Lead requirements-gathering conversations with customers to understand their data structures, workflows, and reporting needs.
Translate customer requirements into well-structured data configurations and reports that align with their business goals.
Build and deliver clear reports and dashboards for customers that surface key insights from their imported data.
Communicate proactively with customers to clarify data formats, align on timelines, and keep stakeholders informed throughout the onboarding process.
Leverage AI tools to automate and templatize repeatable workflows.


What we’re looking for:

Don’t meet every single requirement? At Affinity, we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you’re excited about this role, we encourage you to apply anyways.


Excel / Google Sheets power user — comfortable with VLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH, pivot tables, and text manipulation.
Strong client communication skills, with the ability to lead requirements-gathering conversations, ask the right questions, and translate business needs into technical solutions.
Confident communicator who can present data findings and reports to non-technical stakeholders in a clear, concise way.
Strong attention to detail with a quality-first mindset.
Familiarity with CRM platforms or database concepts (fields, records, relationships).
Good communication skills.
Organized and able to manage multiple imports with competing deadlines.
Manage the data import queue with discipline — tracking status, ETA, priority, and next steps for every import.
Experience with data analytics, reporting, or dashboard building (Looker, Tableau, or similar).


Nice to have:


Experience in B2B SaaS, customer onboarding, or professional services.


About Affinity

With more than 3,000 customers worldwide and backed by some of Silicon Valley's best firms, Affinity has raised $120M to empower dealmakers to find, manage, and close more deals. How? Our Relationship Intelligence platform uses the wealth of data exhaust from trillions of interactions between Investment Bankers, Venture Capitalists, Consultants, and other strategic dealmakers to deliver automated relationship insights that drive over 450,000 deals every month. We are are proud to have received Inc. and Fortune Best Workplaces awards as well as to be Great Places to Work certified for the last 5 years running. Join us on our mission to make it possible for anyone to cultivate and fully harness their network to succeed.

Title: Data Engineering & Analytics Associate
Description:
Who we are




Metyis is a global and forward-thinking firm operating across a wide range of industries, developing and delivering Big Data, Digital Commerce, Marketing & Design solutions and Advisory services. At Metyis, our long-term partnership model brings long-lasting impact and growth to our business partners and clients through extensive execution capabilities.

With our team, you can experience a collaborative environment with highly skilled multidisciplinary experts, where everyone has room to build bigger and bolder ideas. Being part of Metyis means you can speak your mind and be creative with your knowledge. Imagine the things you can achieve with a team that encourages you to be the best version of yourself.




We are Metyis. Partners for Impact.




What we offer




Interact with senior stakeholders at our clients on regular basis to drive their business towards impactful change
Become the go-to person for end-to-end data handling, management, and analytics processes
Working with Data Scientists to take data throughout its lifecycle - acquisition, exploration, data cleaning, integration, analysis, interpretation and visualization
Become part of a fast-growing international and diverse team




What you will do




Translate business problems into structured analytical approaches and data solutions
Analyse structured and unstructured data to identify insights and opportunities
Build and maintain ETL/ELT pipelines, datasets, and scalable data-processing solutions
Develop automation and analytical solutions using SQL, Python, Scala, or similar tools
Perform data-quality checks, validation, and performance optimisation
Support reporting, advanced analytics, and machine learning use cases
Collaborate with data science, engineering, reporting, product, and business teams
Communicate findings clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders
Manage deliverables across multiple projects and clients




What you´ll bring




3–6 years of experience in data engineering, analytics, data science, or a hybrid data role
Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, or a related field
Strong SQL skills and good programming knowledge in Python, Scala, or another scripting language
Experience with ETL/ELT pipelines, databases, data warehouses, and data modelling
Ability to analyse data and translate findings into business recommendations
Working knowledge of statistics and machine learning
Experience with Azure, AWS, or GCP
Familiarity with Git and CI/CD is desirable
Strong problem-solving, business understanding, and stakeholder-management skills
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English and Spanish




At Metyis, we are driven by curiosity and collaboration. We value diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging (DEIB) in all its forms as it makes us stronger as an organisation and promotes creativity and innovation. We welcome all talents and are committed to creating a workplace where every employee can make a meaningful impact and grow.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Guesty is the all-in-one platform helping hospitality businesses around the world automate, optimize, and scale their operations. We’re a fast-growing global startup transforming the industry — from enhancing guest experiences to driving business growth.

With a powerful suite of features and integrations with 150+ industry partners, including Airbnb, Vrbo, Booking.com, Expedia, and Google Travel, Guesty empowers property managers to deliver exceptional guest experiences while running efficient, data-driven operations.

Today, we’re proud to have 800+ team members across 16 countries, all working together to shape the future of hospitality technology.

We are looking for a skilled Data Analyst to join our Analytics team. In this role, you will support the Marketing organization by delivering actionable insights, monitoring campaign performance, and developing data-driven recommendations that improve business decisions. You will own marketing dashboards, define key performance metrics, and partner with stakeholders to optimize acquisition, engagement, and overall marketing effectiveness.

Responsibilities:

Marketing Analytics


Serve as the primary analytics partner for the Marketing team.
Analyze marketing performance across paid search, paid social, email, organic, and other acquisition channels.
Provide insights that support strategic and operational decision-making.


Reporting & Dashboard Development


Build, maintain, and improve marketing dashboards.
Develop scalable reporting solutions that provide accurate and timely business insights.
Ensure data quality and reporting consistency.


KPI Monitoring & Performance Analysis


Define, monitor, and report on key marketing KPIs, including CAC, LTV, and ROAS.
Identify trends, opportunities, and risks using quantitative analysis.
Present findings and recommendations to senior stakeholders.


Data Management


Use SQL to extract, transform, and analyze data from multiple sources.
Support the continuous improvement of marketing data models and analytics frameworks.
Maintain reliable and accurate reporting pipelines.


Cross-functional Collaboration


Partner with Marketing leadership and cross-functional teams to understand business needs.
Translate complex analytical findings into clear, actionable recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.


Requirements:

Experience


Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Economics, Mathematics, Physics, Industrial Engineering, or another quantitative discipline.
2+ years of experience as a Data Analyst or Business Analyst, preferably within a SaaS environment.
Experience supporting marketing analytics initiatives.


Technical Skills


Advanced SQL proficiency.
Strong knowledge of data visualization and reporting best practices.

Guesty is the all-in-one platform helping hospitality businesses around the world automate, optimize, and scale their operations. We’re a fast-growing global startup transforming the industry — from enhancing guest experiences to driving business growth.

With a powerful suite of features and integrations with 150+ industry partners, including Airbnb, Vrbo, Booking.com, Expedia, and Google Travel, Guesty empowers property managers to deliver exceptional guest experiences while running efficient, data-driven operations.

Today, we’re proud to have 800+ team members across 16 countries, all working together to shape the future of hospitality technology.

We are looking for a skilled Data Analyst to join our Analytics team. In this role, you will support the Marketing organization by delivering actionable insights, monitoring campaign performance, and developing data-driven recommendations that improve business decisions. You will own marketing dashboards, define key performance metrics, and partner with stakeholders to optimize acquisition, engagement, and overall marketing effectiveness.

Responsibilities:

Marketing Analytics

  • Serve as the primary analytics partner for the Marketing team.
  • Analyze marketing performance across paid search, paid social, email, organic, and other acquisition channels.
  • Provide insights that support strategic and operational decision-making.

Reporting & Dashboard Development

  • Build, maintain, and improve marketing dashboards.
  • Develop scalable reporting solutions that provide accurate and timely business insights.
  • Ensure data quality and reporting consistency.

KPI Monitoring & Performance Analysis

  • Define, monitor, and report on key marketing KPIs, including CAC, LTV, and ROAS.
  • Identify trends, opportunities, and risks using quantitative analysis.
  • Present findings and recommendations to senior stakeholders.

Data Management

  • Use SQL to extract, transform, and analyze data from multiple sources.
  • Support the continuous improvement of marketing data models and analytics frameworks.
  • Maintain reliable and accurate reporting pipelines.

Cross-functional Collaboration

  • Partner with Marketing leadership and cross-functional teams to understand business needs.
  • Translate complex analytical findings into clear, actionable recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.

Requirements:

Experience

  • Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Economics, Mathematics, Physics, Industrial Engineering, or another quantitative discipline.
  • 2+ years of experience as a Data Analyst or Business Analyst, preferably within a SaaS environment.
  • Experience supporting marketing analytics initiatives.

Technical Skills

  • Advanced SQL proficiency.
  • Strong knowledge of data visualization and reporting best practices.
  • Experience with Looker or similar BI platforms.

Soft Skills

  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Strong communication and presentation abilities.
  • Ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences.
  • Highly organized with strong attention to detail.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with HubSpot, Google Analytics, Google Ads, and Meta Ads Manager.
  • Experience in B2B SaaS.
  • Background in statistical research or advanced analytics.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Como uno de nuestros Data Scientists de Juegos

Serás una de las personas clave que ayuda a transformar preguntas de negocio en insights accionables. Investigarás y analizarás el comportamiento de los jugadores para generar recomendaciones basadas en datos. Para ello, escucharás a los distintos miembros del equipo, comprenderás el contexto y cuestionarás ideas de negocio cuando sea necesario.

Nuestros Data Scientists utilizan una amplia variedad de técnicas, como estadística frecuentista y bayesiana, machine learning, análisis exploratorio y explicativo de datos, inferencia causal, visualización de datos, modelización Monte Carlo, análisis econométrico, entre otras. La amplitud de estas responsabilidades requiere una gran capacidad de aprendizaje, eficiencia en el trabajo colaborativo y habilidad para comunicar datos e insights de forma clara y efectiva.

Los Data Scientists de Juegos son profesionales que impulsan la toma de decisiones de negocio mediante el uso de datos y análisis profundos.

Tendrás la oportunidad de trabajar con conjuntos de datos grandes y complejos, disfrutando de la autonomía necesaria para generar un impacto significativo en el éxito de nuestros juegos. Además, formarás parte de un equipo experimentado y altamente cualificado de aproximadamente 20 profesionales, con múltiples oportunidades de aprendizaje y desarrollo.

Qué harás
Colaborar con los stakeholders para entender y definir los requisitos de análisis.
Realizar análisis exploratorios de datos y convertir los hallazgos en preguntas que puedan responderse mediante técnicas analíticas.
Utilizar analítica avanzada, métodos estadísticos, inferencia causal, machine learning u otras técnicas para responder preguntas y resolver problemas de negocio.
Comunicar los resultados de forma clara y efectiva.
Gestionar requisitos poco definidos o ambiguos.
Explicar análisis complejos e ideas técnicas de manera sencilla y comprensible.
Trabajar de forma autónoma en proyectos complejos.
Mantener una actitud de aprendizaje continuo, adquiriendo nuevas habilidades y metodologías relacionadas con la ciencia de datos, la comunicación o la gestión de stakeholders.
Utilizar una amplia variedad de herramientas y enfoques de ciencia de datos.


Qué buscamos
Titulación universitaria o experiencia equivalente en un área orientada a los datos.
Capacidad para utilizar técnicas de visualización de datos con el fin de comunicar análisis e insights de manera efectiva.
Experiencia utilizando alguna de las siguientes disciplinas para responder preguntas de negocio o científicas: estadística, matemáticas, machine learning, econometría, inferencia causal, modelización Monte Carlo, entre otras.
Experiencia trabajando con R y/o Python.
Conocimientos y experiencia en SQL.


¿Por qué Product Madness?

Como parte de la familia Aristocrat, compartimos su misión de llenar de alegría la vida gracias al poder del juego, con un equipo de primer nivel que crea títulos líderes y de mayor recaudación en el género de casino social, entre ellos Heart of Vegas, Lightning Link y Cashman Casino.

Con un equipo global de 800 personas, Product Madness tiene su sede en Londres, y oficinas en Barcelona, Gdańsk, Lviv, Montreal y un equipo remoto en Estados Unidos, lo que nos convierte en una auténtica potencia global.

Nos guiamos por nuestro principio de “Las personas primero”. Independientemente de dónde, cuándo o cómo trabajen, los miembros de nuestro equipo tienen oportunidades para avanzar en su carrera profesional y crecer con nosotros. Nos enorgullece fomentar una cultura inclusiva, en la que se anima a cada persona a dar lo mejor de sí misma cada día.

Pero no confíes solo en nuestra palabra. En 2024, fuimos incluidos en la lista de los Global Inspiring Workplace Awards y recibimos un premio de bronce en los Stevies for Great Employers, en la categoría de “Empleador del año - Medios de comunicación y entretenimiento”.

Disponibilidad para viajar

Ninguna

Información adicional

En este momento, no podemos tramitar visados de trabajo para este puesto. Los candidatos deben tener permiso para trabajar a tiempo completo en el país donde se ofrece la vacante, sin necesidad de visado ahora ni en el futuro.

Title: AI/BI Analyst
Description:
What You'll Do:


Proactively identify and collect analytical, tooling and reporting needs from different stakeholders – Finance, Revenue Operations, FP&A,...
Support, develop and maintain self-service automated reporting for multiple teams across the organization using Power BI, AI tools or any other relevant technologies
Work collaboratively with key stakeholders (especially IIT) to continuously improve customer centricity, enhance user functionality and maximize reporting outputs
Stay up-to-date with the latest AI & BI tools and technologies, and make recommendations for new tools and approaches that could improve our BI capabilities
Monitor and maintain current reporting processes & data integrity that are relying on data from different systems and platforms
Incorporate feature requests or new tools into product roadmap based on leadership / end users business needs in order to optimize our processes
Provide support on a daily basis to finance & business teams on any issues/blockers they might face
Coordinate training to end users on new reporting tools, processes and best practices
Develop strong project management skills to support our growth and organizational changes



Who You Are:


4-5 years of experience working within AI, BI, Data analytics or/and Finance
Bachelor’s degree or higher in a quantitative field (Mathematics, Economics, Engineering, Computer Science etc.) or in Business, Finance with equivalent practical experience
SQL proficiency is required
Experience working with Power BI & enterprise AI platforms/tools e.g. Dust
Proven experience building and scaling agentic AI solutions, with a track record of driving automation, efficiency, and speed through the continuous evolution of intelligent systems.
Background in any of the following is a plus: Python, Tableau, Vertica, Hive/Hadoop, MS SQL Studio
Previous Databricks experience is a big plus
Keen intellectual curiosity and ability to structure & solve complex problems in an autonomous way
Exceptional attention to detail coupled with an ability to see the big picture
Thorough conceptual and practical understanding of relational databases, data architecture/governance, and real-world application of statistical concepts
Combining technical skills, passion for learning and the ability to interact with a diverse group of stakeholders in a dynamic environment
Excellent communication skills in English



We acknowledge that many candidates may not meet every single role requirement listed above. If your experience looks a little different from our requirements but you believe that you can still bring value to the role, we’d love to see your application!

Who We Are:

We’re Criteo, the Commerce Intelligence Platform. Criteo helps businesses turn shopper signals into commerce outcomes while delivering more relevant experiences for shoppers. We use proprietary commerce intelligence and AI decisioning to drive relevance for shoppers and performance for businesses.

At Criteo, our culture is as unique as it is diverse. From our offices across the globe or from the comfort of home, our 3,600 Criteos collaborate together to build an open, impactful, and forward-thinking environment.

We foster a workplace where everyone is valued, and employment decisions are based solely on skills, qualifications, and business needs—never on non-job-related factors or legally protected characteristics.

What We Offer:

🏢 Ways of working – Our hybrid model blends home with in-office experiences, making space for both.

📈 Grow with us – Learning, mentorship & career development programs.

💪 Your wellbeing matters – Health benefits, wellness perks & mental health support.

🤝 A team that cares – Diverse, inclusive, and globally connected.

💸 Fair pay & perks – Attractive salary, with performance-based rewards and family-friendly policies, plus the potential for equity depending on role and level.

Additional benefits may vary depending on the country where you work and the nature of your employment with Criteo.

Title: Data & Analytics Specialist
Description:
At Bayer we’re visionaries, driven to solve the world’s toughest challenges and striving for a world where ,Health for all, Hunger for none’ is no longer a dream, but a real possibility. We’re doing it with energy, curiosity and sheer dedication, always learning from unique perspectives of those around us, expanding our thinking, growing our capabilities and redefining ‘impossible’. There are so many reasons to join us. If you’re hungry to build a varied and meaningful career in a community of brilliant and diverse minds to make a real difference, there’s only one choice.

Data & Analytics Specialist

Purpose:

The Data & Analytics Specialist is a hands-on operational role responsible for supporting the reliable, accurate, and timely flow of data that powers business decisions, commercial intelligence, and AI enablement. The role focuses on the execution of data quality tasks, vendor feed monitoring, reporting support, business rule maintenance, and process documentation — contributing to the team's ability to deliver trusted, activation-ready data at scale.

This role represents the evolving business data analyst — moving beyond static reporting toward ensuring data flows, quality, and structure are robust enough to support both human decision-making and AI-driven intelligence. The specialist supports hands-on data work (analytics requests, business rule updates, issue resolution, vendor feed coordination) while building capability in making data AI-ready and leveraging AI tools in daily operations. They develop business context — learning what the data means commercially — while building the technical fluency to work across platforms, taxonomies, and data pipelines.

This is not a traditional reporting role. It is a role for someone who recognizes that the value chain of data is shifting — from "pull a report" to "ensure the machine can interpret, validate, and surface insight autonomously." The specialist supports ensuring data is structured, documented, governed, and contextually enriched so that both BI dashboards and AI products deliver trusted, actionable outputs. They are developing the ability to move fluidly between resolving a data quality issue, monitoring a vendor delivery, supporting a Power BI report, or preparing data for an AI model — and they are building the business understanding to know why each task matters commercially.

This role operates with an AI-aware mindset. Tasks — from data validation to documentation — are executed with growing awareness of how they enable or constrain AI tools downstream. The specialist learns that clean, well-structured, semantically rich data is the fuel for AI-powered products and begins to shape their own work to maximize AI readiness. This means developing the habit of thinking beyond "is this data correct?" toward "can a machine interpret this data accurately and consistently?"

As organizations transition from manual, request-driven analytics to self-service, AI-augmented intelligence, this role contributes to the operational backbone that makes that transition possible — supporting data feeds, taxonomy updates, remediation workflows, and AI-ready datasets under the guidance of senior team members.

Scope (global, regional or local):

Global /Local

Key Tasks/ What You'll Do


Support the coordination and monitoring of data deliveries from self-service analytics (SSA) platforms and external vendors — checking timeliness, completeness, and format compliance — and escalating gaps, anomalies, or issues to senior team members.
Maintain and update business rules, mapping logic, taxonomies, and classification standards under guidance — ensuring they reflect current market realities and are structured for both human and machine consumption.
Investigate and help resolve data quality issues — supporting root cause analysis, implementing corrective actions, and documenting resolutions for future prevention.
Support analytics and BI reporting requests — preparing data, validating outputs, assisting with Power BI dashboard maintenance, and ensuring reports reflect accurate and timely information.
Assist with local market data requests and ad-hoc analytical needs — acting as a supporting link between market teams, global data platforms, and cross-functional stakeholders.
Flag opportunities to transition recurring manual requests into self-service reporting or automated delivery.
Prepare and structure data for AI use cases — ensuring datasets are clean, well-labeled, and documented with the context AI models need to interpret them accurately, with growing consideration for how attributes will be consumed by AI models.
Support the operationalization of AI products — assisting with data validation, output testing, and feedback loops under the direction of senior specialists.
Use AI tools (e.g., Copilot, GenAI assistants, n8n, automation scripts) in daily work to increase efficiency and reduce manual effort — developing fluency in AI-augmented operations.
Identify potential automation opportunities within data operations — flagging where AI or rule-based automation could replace repetitive manual tasks in mapping, validation, and quality checks.
Create and maintain operational documentation — including process SOPs, mapping logic, vendor feed specifications, and troubleshooting guides — written with clarity and structure that supports both human users and AI system context layers.
Support onboarding of new team members and contribute to continuous improvement by flagging recurring issues and suggesting process enhancements.


Key Working Relation:


Global / Local Team
Local/Regional Business Teams & Stakeholders
IT / Data Engineering & Data Science teams
External Data Providers (e.g., IQVIA, NielsenIQ, GfK, Euromonitor)
Commercial Analytics & BI users
AI / Digital Innovation teams


Qualification & Competencies:


Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, Data Analytics, Information Management, or related field.
1–3 years of experience in data operations, data quality, business analytics support, or commercial data analytics — experience in FMCG, Consumer Health, or Pharmaceuticals is a plus but not required.
Some exposure to syndicated market data (IQVIA, NielsenIQ, GfK) or willingness to learn — including familiarity with data structures, delivery formats, and common quality issues.
Basic experience or understanding of data mapping rules, product matching logic, and classification standards.
Good analytical and problem-solving skills — ability to investigate data issues methodically and support resolution.
Proficiency in Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables, data manipulation); good knowledge of SQL, Power BI, or similar tools is a plus.
Strong personal drive to explore, test, and use AI tools, systems, and automation approaches in daily work — with a proactive habit of experimenting and learning.
Interest in automation concepts (e.g., scripting basics, workflow tools, AI-assisted data processes, prompt engineering fundamentals).
Detail-oriented with good organizational skills and ability to manage multiple tasks with appropriate prioritization.
Ability to create clear process documentation and follow established SOPs.
Good communicator — able to work across cultures and functions, explain data issues clearly to colleagues and stakeholders.
Self-motivated, reliable, and comfortable working in a global, matrixed environment with remote collaboration.
Growth mindset — views AI as a learning opportunity and actively seeks to integrate AI into personal workflows.
Fluent English required; additional languages a plus.


YOUR APPLICATION

This is your opportunity to tackle the world’s biggest challenges with us: Maintaining our health, feeding growing populations and slowing the rate of climate change. You have a voice, ideas and perspectives and we want to hear them. Because our success begins with you. Be part of something big. Be Bayer.

Bayer welcomes applications from all individuals, regardless of race, national origin, gender, age, physical characteristics, social origin, disability, union membership, religion, family status, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression or any unlawful criterion under applicable law. We are committed to treating all applicants fairly and avoiding discrimination.

Location:

Spain : Cataluña : Barcelona

Division:

Enabling Functions

Reference Code:

875473

  • External Data Providers (e.g., IQVIA, NielsenIQ, GfK, Euromonitor)
  • Commercial Analytics & BI users
  • AI / Digital Innovation teams

Qualification & Competencies:

  • Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, Data Analytics, Information Management, or related field.
  • 1–3 years of experience in data operations, data quality, business analytics support, or commercial data analytics — experience in FMCG, Consumer Health, or Pharmaceuticals is a plus but not required.
  • Some exposure to syndicated market data (IQVIA, NielsenIQ, GfK) or willingness to learn — including familiarity with data structures, delivery formats, and common quality issues.
  • Basic experience or understanding of data mapping rules, product matching logic, and classification standards.
  • Good analytical and problem-solving skills — ability to investigate data issues methodically and support resolution.
  • Proficiency in Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables, data manipulation); good knowledge of SQL, Power BI, or similar tools is a plus.
  • Strong personal drive to explore, test, and use AI tools, systems, and automation approaches in daily work — with a proactive habit of experimenting and learning.
  • Interest in automation concepts (e.g., scripting basics, workflow tools, AI-assisted data processes, prompt engineering fundamentals).
  • Detail-oriented with good organizational skills and ability to manage multiple tasks with appropriate prioritization.
  • Ability to create clear process documentation and follow established SOPs.
  • Good communicator — able to work across cultures and functions, explain data issues clearly to colleagues and stakeholders.
  • Self-motivated, reliable, and comfortable working in a global, matrixed environment with remote collaboration.
  • Growth mindset — views AI as a learning opportunity and actively seeks to integrate AI into personal workflows.
  • Fluent English required; additional languages a plus.

YOUR APPLICATION

This is your opportunity to tackle the world’s biggest challenges with us: Maintaining our health, feeding growing populations and slowing the rate of climate change. You have a voice, ideas and perspectives and we want to hear them. Because our success begins with you. Be part of something big. Be Bayer.

Bayer welcomes applications from all individuals, regardless of race, national origin, gender, age, physical characteristics, social origin, disability, union membership, religion, family status, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression or any unlawful criterion under applicable law. We are committed to treating all applicants fairly and avoiding discrimination.

Title: Finance Data Analyst
Description:
As you contemplate your future, you might be asking yourself, what's the next step? Start your journey with us!

We are now looking for a Data Analyst for Finance to join our team in Barcelona (hybrid). The Finance Data Analytics team has been transformative during the last two years by helping our stakeholders succeed in their mission. If you're ready to soar, we're ready to take you there.

Why eDreams ODIGEO

Join the world's leading travel subscription platform

and one of the largest e-commerce businesses in Europe.


Millions of customers every year across 44 markets – 5 brands – 7.7M members in Prime since launching in 2017.
More than 100 million searches per day on our websites - more than 6 billion AI daily predictions
Over 1700 employees – More than 60 different nationalities - 99% permanent contracts - 36.3 average age



We're the world’s leading travel subscription platform. We pioneered Prime, the first and largest travel subscription program, which has topped over 7.7 million members since launching in 2017. Prime members are subscribed to global travel, gaining access to a comprehensive multi-product offering for all their travel needs—including hotels, rail, flights, dynamic packages and car rental, among others— compounded by industry-leading flexibility features and exclusive, member-only benefits. This entire Prime experience is powered by a proprietary, industry-leading AI platform that delivers a smarter, hyper-personalized service and comprehensive travel experience globally to its members.

What you will do:

The Role's Key Responsibilities and Tasks

As an eDOer, you will have clear objectives, great challenges and a clear overview of how your work contributes to the global company project and its customers. As a Finance Data Analyst you will be in charge of:


Responsible for execution of key analysis, being the data entry point for the business team and including taking ownership of the different data sources from the business side. Understand the business needs, gather the data and present the key insights: “Contribute to the Finance strategic roadmap with numbers”
Elaborate business cases to support strategic decisions
Create dashboards and tracking: master the internal Business Intelligence tools to contribute to the monitoring and tracking of our business KPIs. Work with data engineering and data science teams to be able to leverage all our internal data
Contribute to the delivery of Finance strategic projects: participate in the delivery of the key internal projects, giving support from the business side to ensure progress and effective delivery of the desired outcomes.



what do you need to succeed:

Good to have

Bring your unique perspective, speak up, and offer disruptive solutions. You'll have the opportunity to learn and grow while making a real impact on our team. Here's what you need to succeed:


Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative discipline (Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, etc.),
2-4 years experience in a role with strong analytical component
Strong knowledge of SQL is a requirement,
Python programming skills (mostly for data extraction and data manipulation),
Advanced Office skills. Strong experience with analytical tools, ideally with the Google toolset (spreadsheets, google docs, etc)
Strong analytical skills, fast-learner, with high attention to detail and focused on results and productivity
Business Proficiency in English and fluent in Spanish
A self-starter and team player, who also enjoys having fun!



It would be a plus if you have:


Google Analytics knowledge (as well as Looker Studio),
Previous experience in programming,
Knowledge on data structure and data sets management (as Google Cloud Platform),
SAP knowledge.



Preferred Qualifications


Experience with Applied AI Tools: Demonstrated comfort using practical AI tools such as Gemini, or other AI-powered assistants.
Experimentation Mindset: Curiosity and eagerness to explore, experiment with, and integrate emerging AI-driven solutions into workflows.
AI-Enhanced Problem Solving: Ability to effectively leverage AI tools to streamline processes, and enhance productivity.
Adaptability and Learning Agility: Enthusiastic about continuously learning and quickly adapting to new AI features and capabilities.



and what's in it for you?

The best talent deserves the best benefits

At eDO, we want you to be a part of our success story and great culture. Here's what we offer:


A rewarding Compensation package! 💥 Prime Plus membership, competitive salary and benefits package, including flexible benefits, performance-based bonuses, birthday day off, discounts and partnerships, relocation support and the possibility of choosing your equipment and, even better, keeping it for free after 3 years.
Continuous learning to fuel your growth and explore new horizons! 📚 Learn and grow with free Coursera access, soft skills workshops, tech training, leadership development, and more. Plus, enjoy a great onboarding program.
Grow opportunities to empower your career, and unleash your potential! 🚀 Personalised career paths and the eVOLVE Program will help you discover, grow, and thrive. Internal mobility opportunities let you pursue horizontal career changes and promotions.
Your Well-being is our priority. Embrace Freedom and Flexibility! 💙 At eDO, we value flexibility, employee care, and transparency. We offer a hybrid home-office model focused on outcome, not time-in-seat. You'll be able to find the right work-personal life balance that suits you best.
Work hard, party hard! We believe in having fun and connecting with colleagues! 🎉 Join eDO for after-work events, paddle tournaments, parties, and more. Create communities based on your passions, like sports and music. Come to work as you are, with no dress code, and enjoy free fruit, coffee, and tea at our offices.
Enjoy a dynamic and healthy environment! 💫 Be innovative, take risks, and share your ideas. Our diverse and open-minded teams support high performance, learning, and growth. You'll work in an Agile mindset environment with recognition at our core.



Wanna take a peek into what it's like to work at eDO? Follow us on LinkedIn and discover more about life at eDO!

If you are ready for a career opportunity with unmatched benefits, continuous learning, and a supportive work-life balance, look no further! Take your career to new destinations by applying now and help our diverse, inclusive, and passionate team shape the future of travel.

We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status or disability status. Our people are the key of our success. Each one of them is unique in their own way and the respect for their uniqueness is and will always be a passion and motivation. Don’t meet every requirement? If you’re excited about this role but you do not feel your experience aligns perfectly with all the requirements we strongly encourage you to apply nevertheless.

Apply now!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
El marketing digital ha dejado de ser una línea en un presupuesto para convertirse en el motor de crecimiento de las marcas. Pero entre tanta herramienta, dato y canal, muchas empresas necesitan un aliado que conecte todas las piezas: estrategia, tecnología, ejecución y resultados. Adsmurai se ha convertido en ese aliado.

Somos un Growth-Tech Partner. Combinamos tecnología propia, datos, inteligencia artificial y criterio experto para convertir el marketing en un sistema de crecimiento escalable. Diseñamos, construimos y optimizamos el sistema completo que hace que una marca crezca mejor, porque creemos que el crecimiento no viene de hacer más campañas, sino de conectar mejor las piezas.

Nuestra plataforma, Adsmurai Marketing Platform, es el cerebro de todo. Centraliza la gestión de campañas, datos, audiencias y feeds, permitiendo a marcas y agencias escalar resultados mediante estrategias automatizadas e inteligentes. Integra múltiples fuentes, plataformas y KPIs en tiempo real, automatiza catálogos y creatividades, y analiza datos de e-commerce, CRM y medios para optimizar la eficacia publicitaria.

Con una cultura basada en la excelencia, la agilidad y la colaboración, impulsamos un entorno donde la innovación audaz y la eficiencia generan resultados transformadores. Nuestro equipo, formado por más de 460 expertos, es clave en esta revolución tecnológica.

Somos partner oficial de Meta, Google, Pinterest y TikTok, y referente para marcas líderes en retail-fashion, lujo, educación y deportes. Contamos con oficinas en Barcelona, Málaga, Bogotá y Madrid, estableciendo el estándar global de eficiencia y excelencia en marketing digital.

🔍Buscamos un/a Data Analyst con un fuerte componente de consultoría estratégica, implementación técnica y comunicación con cliente.

🚀 Desafíos Del Puesto


Estrategia y gestión de analítica digital: Liderar los proyectos de analítica digital de punta a punta, convirtiendo los objetivos de negocio de nuestros clientes en planes de medición sólidos que impulsen su crecimiento y toma de decisiones.
Implementación y auditoría avanzada: Poner a punto y auditar cuentas de GA4 y GTM. Te encargarás de resolver problemas complejos de pérdida de datos, atribución, tráfico, configuraciones cross-domain, y personalización de agrupaciones de canales, entre otros.
Modelado y extracción de datos (BigQuery): Explotar el dato crudo de GA4. Te encargarás de realizar consultas en SQL dentro de BigQuery para limpiar, transformar y conectar el comportamiento web con otras fuentes de datos del cliente.
Visualización de datos y data storytelling: Construir cuadros de mando e informes de exploración avanzados en herramientas de BI (Looker, Looker Studio, Power BI). El objetivo es traducir datos complejos en paneles intuitivos que permitan a los clientes tomar decisiones estratégicas de negocio.
Optimización de la conversión (CRO): Analizar en profundidad los funnels de venta y comportamiento del usuario. Utilizarás herramientas de heatmaps y grabaciones para identificar fricciones, y diseñarás, ejecutarás e interpretarás tests A/B para mejorar la experiencia de usuario y la tasa de conversión.
Gobernanza del dato y privacidad: Garantizar la calidad y consistencia del dato recolectado, asegurando que las implementaciones respeten las normativas vigentes y configurando herramientas de privacidad


💯 Eres tu si:


Experiencia sólida en Analítica Digital: Tienes un recorrido demostrable gestionando de punta a punta el ecosistema de Google Analytics 4 (GA4) y Google Tag Manager (GTM), idealmente tanto en entornos web como en aplicaciones móviles.
Dominio de SQL y BigQuery: No te limitas a la interfaz gráfica de las herramientas; sabes cómo interrogar bases de datos en la nube mediante SQL para extraer análisis mucho más granulares y avanzados.
Experto en herramientas de BI: Tienes experiencia avanzada conectando, modelando y visualizando datos en plataformas como Looker Studio (Data Studio), Power BI o Looker.
Perfil analítico con enfoque CRO: Posees mentalidad de experimentación. Sabes utilizar y extraer conclusiones de herramientas cualitativas (Hotjar, Microsoft Clarity) y plataformas de A/B testing (VWO/AB Tasty, Optimizely, o similares).
Habilidades de consultoría y comunicación: Eres capaz de sentarte con un cliente o stakeholder, entender sus necesidades de negocio, traducirlas a requisitos técnicos y, posteriormente, explicar los resultados de forma sencilla y accionable.
Server-Side Tagging: Experiencia o conocimientos implementando mediciones a través de Google Tag Manager Server-Side.
Conocimientos de Privacidad (Consent Mode): Experiencia configurando el modo de consentimiento de Google (Consent Mode v2) y gestionando CMPs (Cookiebot, OneTrust, etc.).


✨ Plus Muy Valorado


Integración en CMS y plataformas E-commerce: Experiencia trabajando con CMS estándares (Shopify, WooCommerce, Magento/Adobe Commerce, etc.) y entendimiento de cómo desplegar o conectar la capa de datos (Data Layer) en ellos para una correcta medición de e-commerce.
Analítica de Apps (Firebase y MMPs): Tienes experiencia midiendo aplicaciones móviles. Sabes cómo estructurar y gestionar eventos en Firebase y tienes familiaridad con herramientas de atribución móvil (MMPs como AppsFlyer, Adjust, Branch, etc.) para entender la captación y el ciclo de vida del usuario app.
Dominio de otras herramientas de analítica (Adobe Analytics): Suma puntos si has trabajado en entornos corporativos con Adobe Analytics u otras plataformas alternativas de analítica web/app.
Nociones de desarrollo front-end: Conocimientos básicos de JavaScript, HTML y CSS que te permitan ser autónomo a la hora de crear etiquetas personalizadas o entender fallos en la web.
Entendimiento de ecosistemas Cloud (GCP/Azure): Familiaridad con otros servicios de almacenamiento y procesamiento de datos en la nube.


✨ ¿Qué ofrecemos?


Contrato indefinido.
Flexibilidad horaria de entrada y salida.
Modelo híbrido oficina-remoto con 2 días de teletrabajo.
Jornada intensiva los viernes, prefestivos laborables, julio y agosto.
23 días de vacaciones (+1 día por año de antigüedad).
Adsmubirthday—día libre por tu cumpleaños.
Formación activa dentro de la empresa y crecimiento profesional a través de nuestro Plan de Carrera.
AdsmuAcademy (plataforma de formación adhoc para los trabajadores).
Actividades de equipo y afterworks.
Clases de inglés.
Convenio con gimnasios (WellHub)
Plan de retribución flexible: ticket restaurante, transporte y seguro médico.


🍀 Estos son nuestros valores:


Make things happen: Aplicamos la razón en la toma de decisiones basándonos en datos. Gestionamos de principio a fin y orientándonos a resultados.
Walk, run, fly: Disparamos la proactividad ejecutando ideas. Nos marcamos metas ambiciosas, tenemos curiosidad y no tenemos miedo al fracaso.
Passionate players: Trabajamos con actitud positiva y ponemos pasión en todo lo que hacemos, con perseverancia y orgullo por nuestro trabajo.
Work together: Construimos relaciones de confianza basadas en la honestidad. Escuchamos, empatizamos y respetamos a las personas.


Nuestra cultura está alineada con los valores universales de libertad, cooperación y autorrealización. Trabajamos cada día para que todo el mundo tenga las mismas oportunidades independientemente de su sexo, etnia, nacionalidad, orientación sexual, creencias políticas o religiosas y nunca aprobaremos ninguna acción discriminatoria.

Adsmurai nació en 2014 de una conversación entre amigos y tratamos de mantener y transmitir ese buen rollo y convertirlo en un pilar fundamental de nuestra cultura.

  • Plan de retribución flexible: ticket restaurante, transporte y seguro médico.

🍀 Estos son nuestros valores:

  • Make things happen: Aplicamos la razón en la toma de decisiones basándonos en datos. Gestionamos de principio a fin y orientándonos a resultados.
  • Walk, run, fly: Disparamos la proactividad ejecutando ideas. Nos marcamos metas ambiciosas, tenemos curiosidad y no tenemos miedo al fracaso.
  • Passionate players: Trabajamos con actitud positiva y ponemos pasión en todo lo que hacemos, con perseverancia y orgullo por nuestro trabajo.
  • Work together: Construimos relaciones de confianza basadas en la honestidad. Escuchamos, empatizamos y respetamos a las personas.

Nuestra cultura está alineada con los valores universales de libertad, cooperación y autorrealización. Trabajamos cada día para que todo el mundo tenga las mismas oportunidades independientemente de su sexo, etnia, nacionalidad, orientación sexual, creencias políticas o religiosas y nunca aprobaremos ninguna acción discriminatoria.

Adsmurai nació en 2014 de una conversación entre amigos y tratamos de mantener y transmitir ese buen rollo y convertirlo en un pilar fundamental de nuestra cultura.

Title: Pricing & Business Analyst
Description:
Al Barça som MÉS QUE UN CLUB perquè tenim un ferm propòsit: volem transformar el món a través de la nostra manera d’entendre l’esport, amb la nostra essència Barça, i amb el nostre compromís social.




Quina és la nostra essència?

Tenim un estil propi... quan juguem volem guanyar, però sobretot volem jugar sense renunciar al nostre estil.
Som una escola de vida que forma persones a través de l’esport.
Treballem per la igualtat de gènere en l’esport potenciant els equips femenins.
Creiem en les diferents disciplines, per això competim amb 5 esports professionals diferents.
Treballem amb els millors professionals i experts del món de l’esport, generant nous coneixements que apliquem i compartim.
Sabem que l'esport pot ser una eina de canvi social; per això, els nostres valors inspiren les nostres accions tant dins com fora del terreny de joc.
Som un Club propietat de més de 144.000 socis i sòcies que prenen grans decisions democràticament.
Fomentem la inclusió de la diversitat de manera equitativa entre totes les persones del nostre entorn.




Per tot això, podem fer realitat el nostre propòsit essent capdavanters, amb la nostra personalitat i els nostres valors.




Què busquem?

L’Àrea de Negoci d’Instal·lacions busca incorporar una persona en la posició de Pricing & Business Analyst per donar suport analític avançat a l’àrea de B2C Sports revenue, amb un focus clar en la maximització d’ingressos mitjançant Dynamic Pricing, proporcionant insights accionables sobre el comportament de la demanda amb alta antelació.

La posició té com a objectiu transformar grans volums de dades complexes (partits, zones, preus i timing de venda) en dades i insights de negoci per a l’estratègia de dynamic pricing permetent una gestió proactiva en un context d’alt impacte econòmic, identificat com una de les principals palanques de creixement incremental en esports i actualment amb un ampli marge de millora derivat de la manca de recursos analítics dedicats




Funcions principals:




1. Anàlisi avançada de demanda i comportament de compra




Analitzar corbes de venda per partit, zona i tipologia de client, identificant diferències de rendiment amb mesos d’antelació.
Mesurar velocitat de compra, pics de demanda i finestres òptimes d’ajust de preus.
Analitzar l’impacte de variables clau: rival, calendari, dia/hora, aforament disponible, context competitiu i fase de venda.
Generar i traduir insights analítics en recomanacions de negoci accionables.




2. Dynamic Pricing i elasticitat de preus




Calcular i modelitzar l’ elasticitat preu-demanda per zona (principalment GA, amb extensió progressiva a VIP).
Identificar zones amb baixa sensibilitat al preu i alt potencial d’increment d’ingressos.
Proposar ajustos de preu progressius i escalonats segons el comportament real de compra i la fase temporal.
Definir criteris i regles de dynamic pricing basades en dades per a la seva automatització progressiva.




3. Generació d’insights: Traducció de dades en decisions de negoci

Convertir anàlisis complexes en recomanacions clares, accionables i orientades a impacte econòmic per als Product Managers.
Actuar com a partner de negoci, no només com a analista: focus en valor, execució i resultat.
Mesurar i fer seguiment del uplift real generat per les decisions de pricing implementades.




4. Simulació i escenaris d’ingressos (What-if)

Construir models de simulació per avaluar escenaris alternatius de pricing (pujades, timing, intensitat).
Estimar l’impacte en ingressos, volum de vendes i ocupació de l’estadi.
Prioritzar zones i partits segons el ROI esperat de les accions de dynamic pricing.
Avaluar escenaris de risc i sensibilitat per possibles desviacions respecte als objectius d’ingressos.




5. Sistema d’alertes i presa de decisions proactiva

Definir benchmarks de demanda esperada per partit i zona.
Establir alertes automàtiques quan la demanda s’accelera o es desaccelera respecte al previst.
Anticipar oportunitats d’increment de preus abans que el mercat les absorbeixi.




Requisits:




Grau o màster en Business Analytics, Economia, Matemàtiques, Estadística, Enginyeria, Data Science o similar

Experiència prèvia:




3–5 anys d’experiència en departaments de negoci o comercial, amb focus en::
Pricing, Revenue Management o Anàlisi de Demanda.
Entorns d’alta complexitat i múltiples variables.
Es valorarà experiència en esports, ticketing, entreteniment, aerolínies, hospitality o turisme, on el pricing dinàmic és crític.




Eines i habilitats:

Anàlisi de dades avançada (Excel, SQL, Python, eines BI).
Modelització d’elasticitats i escenaris d’ingressos.
Capacitat per treballar amb grans volums de dades no estructurades.
Mentalitat orientada a optimització i maximització d’ingressos.
Capacitat per convertir anàlisis complexes en recomanacions aplicables al negoci.
Visió de negoci i perfil clarament comercial
Orientació a resultats i focus en impacte econòmic real.
Capacitat de priorització en entorns complexos i amb múltiples fronts oberts.
Comunicació clara per traslladar insights tècnics a decisions executives.







Al FC BARCELONA garantim processos de selecció lliures de prejudicis i amb igualtat d'oportunitats i no discriminació per raons d'origen, gènere, discapacitat o edat a totes les persones candidates durant el procés de selecció i que seran avaluades d’acord amb els requisits de l’oferta laboral amb transparència.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Who We Are

Let's have fun shaping your future!

We are Socialpoint, a world-renowned mobile game studio proudly part of the Zynga & Take-Two Interactive family. We’re the creators of hits like Dragon City, Two Dots, Top Troops, and Monster Legends, loved by hundreds of millions of players around the globe.We craft the best social games, bringing joy from Barcelona to the world. Now, we want to bring the world to Barcelona! At Socialpoint, work and play intertwine seamlessly, and we celebrate the diversity that makes us stronger. No matter who you are or where you’re from, you’re welcome here. Join us for a future where making games is as thrilling as playing them!

Position Overview

We are looking to welcome yet another brilliant Senior Marketing Data Analyst into our Marketing Analytics team.

As a Senior Data Analyst, you’ll be responsible for designing and driving high-impact analytics solutions, from developing advanced statistical frameworks to evaluate marketing performance, to building data pipelines that integrate third-party sources, to creating interactive tools that empower partner teams. These are just a few examples of the initiatives you’ll help lead and deliver end-to-end, playing a key role in shaping the technical direction of our analytics efforts. The demands on this team are constantly evolving with the needs of Socialpoint’s and Zynga’s dynamic marketing teams, so it is important that you be able to adapt and utilize your time efficiently.

What You'll Do


Partner closely with marketing and cross-functional teams to identify growth opportunities, improve efficiency, and shape strategic initiatives.
Lead analytical deep dives to surface insights and translate them into actionable recommendations for senior stakeholders.
Visualize and communicate analytical findings clearly, enabling data-driven decision-making across the organization.
Apply statistical methodologies to evaluate performance and account for uncertainties in major initiatives.
Proactively explore and prototype opportunities using AI-driven automation to streamline investigations and improve decision-making.


What You Bring


Demonstrated experience with some or all of the following: predictive modeling, statistics, experimental design, optimization, data visualization
Understanding of marketing metrics and KPIs, with experience in campaign optimization, Lifetime Value (LTV) modeling, and incrementality analysis
Proficiency in SQL and Python with hands-on experience in Redshift, Databricks, PySpark, and Airflow
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to translate ambiguous questions into data-driven insights
Strong communication skills — you can explain complex data in simple, actionable terms
Familiarity with AI/ML concepts or experience in automating recurring analytics workflows is a plus
A team player, you establish effective relationships to work with others for the win
Proven work experience in data analytics or data science roles
BS in Computer Science, Math, Statistics, Economics, or other quantitative field; Masters, MBA or PhD preferred


What's in it for you as a Socialpointer?


Hybrid working model: we ask you to come to our Barcelona office at least 2 days per week
A generous 25 days of annual leave, plus public holidays
Private medical insurance for you and your family to keep your health bar full
Disability and life insurance
Choose from the latest iOS or Android device when you join (phone or tablet)
Free language lessons in English, Spanish and Catalan
Spotify Premium to add the perfect soundtrack to your adventure
Legendary social events and parties to let off some steam
Take-Two Interactive employee stock purchase plan (eligibility criteria applies)
Professional development budget for skills training


Onsite Perks Include


State of the art gym facilities
Free breakfast, lunch, and specialty coffee
Onsite physiotherapist and hairdresser (no wallet needed!)


The list goes on and on… so get your game on and apply now! Click “Apply” to level up and submit your application in English.

We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, which means we are committed to creating and celebrating diverse thoughts, cultures, and backgrounds throughout our organization. Employment with us is based on substantive ability, objective qualifications, and work ethic – not an individual’s race, creed, color, religion, sex or gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin or ancestry, alienage or citizenship status, physical or mental disability, pregnancy, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, status as a victim of domestic violence or sex offenses, reproductive health decision, or any other characteristics protected by applicable law.

As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to providing the necessary support and accommodation to qualified individuals with disabilities, health conditions, or impairments (subject to any local qualifying requirements) to ensure their full participation in the job application or interview process. Please contact us at recruitment@socialpoint.es to request any accommodations or for support related to your application for an open position.

Please be aware that Socialpoint does not conduct job interviews or make job offers over third-party messaging apps such as Telegram, WhatsApp, or others. Socialpoint also does not engage in any financial exchanges during the recruitment or onboarding process, and will never ask a candidate for their personal or financial information over an app or other unofficial chat channel. Any attempt to do so may be the result of a scamp or phishing attack, and you should not engage. Socialpoint’s in-house recruitment team will only contact individuals through their official Company email addresses (i.e., via a zynga.com, take2games.com, naturalmotion.com, smallgiantgames.com, themavens.com, gram.gs email domain).

Title: Analytics Engineer (m/w/d)
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
This is an exciting opportunity to join the Software Center of Excellence of Dematic as an Analytics Engineer. As part of this global team of software experts, you will help develop robust organizational capabilities in sales, design, engineering and support to deliver exceptional software to our customers.

As a specialist, you will own end-to-end implementation of analytics engagements with Dematic customers, while driving the services pipeline as well as be a key voice internally for the further development of the analytics services program. This is both a cross-functional role within the organization as well as a key customer interfacing role. A successful candidate will have demonstrated exceptional performance, innovation, creativity and insight in a similar role.

We offer:


Career Development
Competitive Compensation and Benefits
Global Opportunities



Tasks and Qualifications:

Tasks


Works with customer end-users to define analytics requirements and works with internal team to concept, design and deliver solution
Works independently, within teams, and with multiple types of skillsets (business, data architect, other technical resources)
Performs business process analysis, mapping and design
Ensures high quality delivery of software consulting services and overall client satisfaction
Drives development and documentation of services
Displays depth of knowledge to customers during sales-phase while representing breadth and depth of Dematic solutions and expertise
Supports the sales organization and drive pipeline generation of analytics consulting services



Qualifications
The qualifications for the position of Analytics Engineer include proven success in Client Management, Project Management, and Services Delivery. Other important areas of experience and skills include:


Experience working with data visualization and analytics tools such as Tableau, PowerBI, QlikView, or Superset is required
Experience working with business users to concept, generate and deliver analytics solutions, dashboards and reporting
Proficient experience in Microsoft SQL Server, Oracle, and/or Google BigQuerry
Overall knowledge of MHE technologies and warehouse systems or similar domains is preferred
Specific domain experience and knowledge in the logistics and supply chain industries is a plus
Excellent written and verbal communication skills including presentation skills and knowledge of software tools (MS PowerPoint, MS Visio)
A willingness to travel in order to satisfy client needs, typically less than 5%
Experience conducting requirements analysis, meeting with business stakeholders and applying solutions to customer challenges
Working knowledge of advanced analytic tools such as SAS, R, or Python is required
Working knowledge of cloud-based technologies is preferred
At least 3 years of experience in a related role

Title: Product Data Analyst (PDA)- Híbrido, Barcelona
Description:
¿Te interesaría seguir desarrollándote como Product Data Analyst en una empresa líder en su sector, trabajando en entornos ágiles, multidisciplinares y orientados a la toma de decisiones basada en datos?




Desde Oxigent Technologies seleccionamos un/a Product Data Analyst (PDA) para participar en un proyecto internacional orientado a producto, trabajando junto a equipos de negocio, producto e ingeniería en un entorno ágil y colaborativo, ubicado en Barcelona.




En el caso de ser la persona seleccionada, tu misión será actuar como referente de datos dentro de una squad de producto, impulsando la toma de decisiones basada en datos, el análisis del comportamiento de usuario y la mejora continua del producto mediante experimentación y analítica avanzada.




¿Cuáles serán tus funciones principales?




Actuar como referente de datos para un producto específico, liderando las iniciativas relacionadas con análisis y calidad del dato.
Definir y monitorizar KPIs y métricas de éxito alineadas con los objetivos de negocio y producto.
Diseñar, ejecutar y analizar experimentos de A/B Testing para evaluar nuevas funcionalidades y optimizaciones.
Analizar el comportamiento de usuarios e identificar tendencias, oportunidades de crecimiento y puntos de mejora.
Construir dashboards, informes y visualizaciones para facilitar la toma de decisiones.

Contratación indefinida a través de Oxigent.

Title: Data Analyst (m/f/d)
Description:
🔎 Who we are?




Quantion is a Business Digital Transformation services company founded in 2015 and part of the Astek Group group operating in more than 25 countries with over 10,000 employees worldwide.

Our team is specialized in application development, UX/UI, and digital business transformation, focused on building innovative solutions that drive measurable business results.




We combine our teams in cutting-edge technologies and digital deep experiences with the innovation ecosystem, to ensure that we can provide the best solution to face business challenges, from its concept to its implementation.




We are looking for a DATA ANALYST to join our Tech Team.




Key Responsibilities:

Analyze live chat performance using SQL and/or Python (Databricks) across structured metrics and conversational datasets
Identify trends, customer pain points, and behavioral patterns, and translate them into actionable insights
Write advanced queries and data transformations to process large-scale, multi-source datasets
Perform data preparation, exploratory analysis, and reporting automation
Design, build, and maintain Tableau dashboards to track live chat KPIs and enable self-serve insights
Integrate conversational, operational, and customer data to create a holistic view of channel performance
Ensure data quality, consistency, and alignment of KPIs across datasets and teams
Communicate findings effectively to stakeholders, translating analysis into clear business narratives and recommendations
Support OKR tracking by aligning metrics with product and business goals
Collaborate closely with product teams, data engineers, and data scientists within the analytics squad to drive data-informed decisions and improvements




Requirements:

3–5 years of experience in Data Analytics, preferably in digital or customer-facing environments
Strong hands-on expertise in SQL, Python, Databricks
Proven experience building dashboards in Tableau
Ability to work with large, complex datasets across multiple sources
Strong analytical thinking with the ability to translate data into actionable insights
Solid understanding of product analytics and customer journeys
Strong communication skills across technical and non-technical stakeholders
Hands-on, proactive mindset with ownership of end-to-end analytical tasks




Our offering:

🚀 You will grow professionally in a company in constant development and evolution.

📏 You will benefit from a personalized follow-up of your professional projects as close as possible to your ambitions.

📈 You will make a real contribution to the company's development in real time.

🏝️ You will enjoy 23 days of vacation.

🌍 You will join our team: diverse, multicultural and constantly growing.

🔋 You will have days full of good energy and good atmosphere.
