Title: Analista de Dados - Aveiro (M/F/D)
HTML:
Porque precisamos de ti?

Precisamos de um perfil capaz de garantir a análise, consolidação e interpretação de dados comerciais, transformando informação em insights acionáveis que suportem a tomada de decisão e contribuam para a melhoria da performance, rentabilidade e eficiência dos processos comerciais.

Enquanto Analista de Dados, irás assegurar a qualidade da informação ao longo de todo o ciclo, desde a recolha e validação até à sua utilização em relatórios e dashboards de apoio à decisão. Inclui a monitorização de indicadores-chave como vendas, margens, pricing e pipeline, com foco na análise de resultados, identificação de tendências e deteção de oportunidades de melhoria.

Poderás, ainda, participar na gestão e evolução dos sistemas de informação comercial, garantindo fiabilidade dos dados e alinhamento com práticas e métricas do Grupo, em articulação com equipas internacionais. Pressupõe ainda uma contribuição ativa para a melhoria contínua e transformação digital, através da automatização de processos, otimização das análises e promoção de uma cultura orientada a dados.

Esta oferta é para ti?

Educação e Formação

  • Formação Superior em Gestão, Economia, Engenharia, Estatística, Data Analytics ou áreas similares;

Experiência Profissional

  • Experiência em análise de dados (preferencialmente em contexto comercial);
  • Conhecimentos de modelos de dados e análise estatística (valorizado);
  • Experiência em reporting e utilização de ferramentas de BI;
  • Experiência com sistemas CRM/ERP (valorizado);
  • Experiência em Power BI, Salesforce, SAP e Excel;
  • Conhecimentos de Excel avançado;

Competências Pessoais

  • Pensamento analítico e crítico;
  • Orientação para resultados e para o negócio;
  • Capacidade de síntese e resolução de problemas;
  • Rigor, organização e atenção ao detalhe;
  • Flexibilidade e adaptação à mudança;
  • Espírito colaborativo.

Um pouco mais sobre nós

"Making the World a Better Home", é essa a nossa missão e o nosso objetivo, procurando as melhores pessoas para que possam contribui diretamente para o nosso propósito!

Presente em 75 países, a Saint-Gobain é líder mundial em construção sustentável e dispõe de uma ampla gama de soluções com uma fórmula avançada, que responde às mais elevadas exigências técnicas.

A nossa atividade? Concebemos, produzimos e distribuímos materiais e serviços para os mercados da construção e da indústria.

As nossas soluções? Encontram-se em todo o lado na nossa vida quotidiana (edifícios, transportes, infraestruturas), proporcionando conforto e sustentabilidade.

A nossa ambição? Onde quer que esteja, deixe que a sua personalidade única e os nossos valores o guiem todos os dias para tornar o mundo mais sustentável!

_______________________________

A Saint-Gobain Portugal é uma sociedade do Grupo Saint-Gobain, líder nacional, que oferece soluções para a construção e renovação de edifícios. A Saint-Gobain Portugal integra as marcas: Isover - soluções em lã mineral para isolamento térmico, acústico e proteção contra o fogo, Placo® - sistemas de construção interior e exterior à base de gesso para edifícios novos ou em reabilitação e Weber - soluções em argamassas industriais para diferentes aplicações em construção.

Entre as atividades Saint-Gobain Portugal estão soluções para: fachadas (isolamento, revestimento e acabamento), colagem e betumação de cerâmica e pedra natural, impermeabilização, pavimentos, argamassas técnicas, projetos 3D, divisórias interiores (gesso, lá e placas), tetos e argila expandida.

Atualmente a Saint-Gobain Portugal conta com 3 centros de produção, Aveiro, Carregado e Pombal.

Benefícios em te juntares a nós

  • Salário de acordo com a experiência;
  • Bónus anual;
  • Subsídio de alimentação atrativo;
  • PPR;
  • Seguro de saúde;
  • Seguro de vida.

Title: Data Analyst (Engineer)
Description:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Role

Our Data Science team solves complex problems with smart, practical solutions. Data Scientists and Analysts work directly with product teams to uncover insights, guide decisions, and improve how customers experience Revolut. They do this by delivering smart, scalable solutions that move the business forward.

We’re looking for a Data Analyst who can dig into our complex databases, look for the root cause of a problem, and design their own solutions by writing code to implement them.

This is an opportunity to apply your skills while picking up new ones and delivering value for millions of customers around the world.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

What You’ll Be Doing


Understanding our business and its processes through data
Applying this understanding and knowledge of data to help product and services teams
Developing documentation and data governance
Owning the entire ETL process
Designing key metrics to measure different aspects of the business
Creating and maintaining new aggregated views and tables to simplify data querying
Providing clean data sets, modelling data in a way that empowers end users to answer their own questions


What You'll Need


5+ years of experience in an analytical role, creating impactful data-driven solutions
A solid background/education in a quantitative discipline
Great skills with Python, SQL, or other programming languages
Evidence of exceptional mathematical and statistics knowledge


Nice to have


An advanced degree in a core STEM subject
Experience with additional programming languages (Java, Scala, C++, etc.)
School/university Olympic medal competitions in physics, maths, economics, or programming


Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.


Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.


We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Decskill was founded in 2014 as an IT Consulting Company and their main mission is to delivery value through the knowledge. We enable companies to meet the chalenges of digital world by providing our clients with business models that ensure technological capacity, flexibility and agility. We are more than 500 consultants with offices in Lisbon, Porto and Madrid.




DECSKILL operates in 3 main areas:

• DECSKILL TALENT: through which we provide our clients with an extension to their IT teams;

• DECSKILL BOOST: through which we provide our client with software development models to increase capacity and optimize Time-to-Market, where we create and manage teams that deliver according to their needs, at the desired speed;

• DECSKILL CONNECT: through which we provide our client with consulting services, as well as the implementation and management of information technology infrastructures.




Our practice results in the creation of value for our customers, either by delivering qualified and value-added services, or through highly qualified and motivated professionals, as well as technology solutions that allow us to operate and transform the business of our customers.




We are looking for a DATA ANALYST.




Responsabilities:

As a Data Analyst, you will play a vital role in our team. Working directly with one or more of the bank's business areas, you will provide valuable insights to drive business growth, understading their needs, proposing and implementing data-driven solutions (analysis, visualization and automation). You will exercise a lot of problem identification and problem solving skills.
You will have the opportunity to take ownership of business essential analytical projects that involve retrieving, preparing and analysing complex datasets using data/statistical models and best practices in data visualization for the reporting creation, with the goal to produce insights and automated tools that will guide strategic solutions
You will move between technical and non-technical areas and should be able to communicate well with different types of professionals. It is very important to be able to clearly and concisely understand stekeholders’ needs and translate these to our technical team, as well as present the results of complex analysis to our stakeholders.




Requierments:

Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computing, Mathematics, Statistics or related fields
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas)
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas)
Ability to analysze, synthetize and simplify.
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus.
Knowledge in Big Data technology such as Pyspark, Presto and Airflow is a plus
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus




If you’re interested in this job please send your CV in English to carolina.mustra@decskill.com with reference “CM/Data Analyst".




Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being.

Title: Data Collection Analyst (Performance Data)
Description:
Overview

Stats Perform is the market leader in sports tech.  We provide the most trusted sports data to some of the world's biggest organizations, across sports, media, and broadcasting.  

Through the latest AI technologies and machine learning, we combine decades' worth of data with the latest in-game happenings. We then offer coaches, teams, professional bodies, and media channels around the world, access to the very best data, content, and insights.  In turn, improving how sports fans interact with their favourite sports teams and competitions.

How do we add value?


Media outlets add a little magic to their coverage with our stats and graphics packages.
Sportsbooks can offer better predictions and more accurate odds.
The world's top coaches are known to use our data to make critical team decisions.
Sports commentators can engage with fans on a deeper level, using our stories and insights.


Anywhere you find sport, Stats Perform is there.  However, data and tech are only half of the package. We need great people to fuel the engine.

We succeeded thanks to a team of amazing people. They spend their days collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data from a wide range of live sporting events. If you combine this real-time data with our 40-year-old archives, elite journalists, camera operators, copywriters, the latest in AI wizardry, and a host of 'behind the scenes' support staff, you've got all the ingredients to make it a magical experience!

Responsibilities


Deliver live coverage within our highest standard measures.
Post-match checks applied through the correct guideline procedures.
Expert knowledge in Performance soccer definitions and processes.
Adept at making carefully considered decisions having consulted stakeholders and weighed up risks with minimal delays.
High knowledge in betting games processes and definitions.
Responsibility for freelancers: support on re-training, definitions and help to improve KPIs.


Required Qualifications:


Experience as Soccer Performance Analyst.
Excellent organization and communication skills.
English skills.
Complete understanding of collection tools and processes.
Specialist knowledge of worldwide soccer leagues and competitions


Desired Qualifications:


Spanish and Portuguese Skills and other languages.
Specialist knowledge of worldwide soccer leagues and competitions.
Organizes own and teamwork by taking into account deadlines and following the established procedures.
Working collaboratively with a group of people in order to accomplish a task or achieve a goal. Treats all team members in a respectful, courteous and professional manner; supports team despite different points of views or setbacks.
Ability to engage and enable people to perform at their best.
Self–motivated and driven to succeed, focused on results and passionate about the job.
On-going learning and development and ability to apply new knowledge.
Ability to adapt to changing business needs, cultures, conditions and work responsibilities.


Why work at Stats Perform?

We love sports, but we love diverse thinking more!

We know that diversity brings creativity, so we invite people from all backgrounds to join us.  At Stats Perform you can make a difference, by using your skills and experience every day, you'll feel valued and respected for your contribution.  

We take care of our colleagues

We like happy and healthy colleagues. You will benefit from things like Mental Health Days Off, ‘No Meeting Fridays,’ and flexible working schedules.

We pull together to build a better workplace and world for all.

We encourage employees to take part in charitable activities, utilize their 2 days of Volunteering Time Off, support our environmental efforts, and be actively involved in Employee Resource Groups.

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion at Stats Perform

By joining Stats Perform, you'll be part of a team that celebrates diversity. A team that is dedicated to creating an inclusive atmosphere where everyone feels valued and welcome. All employees are collectively responsible for developing and maintaining an inclusive environment. That is why our Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion goals underpin our core values.

With increased diversity comes increased innovation and creativity. Ensuring we're best placed to serve our clients and communities. Stats Perform is committed to seeking diversity, equity, and inclusion in all we do.

Title: People Data Analyst
Description:
Farfetch is a leading global marketplace for the luxury fashion industry, connecting customers in over 190 countries with items from more than 50 countries and over 1,400 of the world’s best brands and charming boutiques from around the world. Farfetch opens a world of luxury for endless expressions of style, delivering a truly unique shopping experience and access to the most extensive selection of luxury on a global marketplace.

PEOPLE TEAM

The People team plays a pivotal role in supporting the business by strategically overseeing the complete employee journey. From drawing in top talent and fostering a high-performance culture and career development, they are instrumental in creating a positive and inclusive workplace environment and managing our workspaces.

PORTO

Our office is near Porto, in the north of Portugal, and is located in a vibrant business hub. It offers a dynamic and welcoming environment where our employees can connect and network with a large community of tech professionals.

ROLE

As part of the People Data & Insights team, you will be a key player in our mission to shape our organizational strategy and empower the business with data-driven insights. We are the analytical and strategic engine that enables the People Team and senior leadership to make smarter, more strategic decisions about our organization and our people. This role will provide you with a deep understanding of the intersection of People Analytics, business strategy, organizational transformation, and process automation. You will be at the center of critical initiatives that drive our long-term vision and operational efficiency.

What You'll Do


Provide Strategic Analysis: Be passionate about optimization and critical thinking. You will analyze complex headcount data and people-related financial numbers, providing key insights for forecasting, budgeting, and identifying efficiency opportunities
Collaborate Across the Business: Liaise with multiple internal and external stakeholders globally, ensuring strategic analysis and project updates are channelled effectively through the business
Champion Process Automation: Identify and analyze existing People processes, designing clean, automated, and scalable solutions, using the most recent tech capabilities (AI-driven) that flow smoothly and unlock more time for high-value work
Transform Data into Actionable Insights: Developing dashboards and reports ensuring their correct communication to all involved stakeholders. You will transform complex data into clear, actionable insights for leadership
Leverage Our HR Tech Stack: Maximize the potential of our core HRIS, Workday, by building and enhancing its native dashboards and reports. Your focus will be on creating intuitive, self-service analytics tools that empower our stakeholders with on-demand data to drive their decisions
Drive Strategic & Transformational Projects: Be a key player in multi-disciplinary, end-to-end project teams to address new business needs, driving continuous improvement and strategic change initiatives related to our people and processes





Who You Are


Degree in Economics, Management, Mathematics, Engineering or other relevant quantitative degree
2-3 years of professional experience mandatory
Fluent English is mandatory
Proficiency in Excel and Google Sheets is mandatory
Experience with BI tools such as Power BI is a strong plus
Familiarity with BigQuery is a plus
Familiarity with AI solutions is a plus
Proven experience in a similar role blending data analysis, project management, and strategic thinking
Strong numerical reasoning, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills
Organized and detail-oriented with a proactive approach to managing projects end-to-end





Rewards & Benefits


Health insurance for the whole family, flexible working environment and well-being support and tools
Extra days off, sabbatical program and days for you to give back for the community
Training opportunities and free access to Udemy
Flexible benefits program





EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES STATEMENT



Farfetch is an equal opportunities employer ensuring that all applicants are treated equally and fairly throughout our recruitment process. We are determined that no applicant experiences discrimination on the basis of sex, race, ethnicity, religion or belief, disability, age, gender identity, ancestry, sexual orientation, veteran status, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law.





SCAM DISCLAIMER



It has come to our attention that there may be fraudulent activities involving individuals or organizations falsely claiming to represent Farfetch in order to attract candidates to a SCAM. Please be aware that Farfetch does not conduct recruitment processes through messaging apps or any unofficial communication channels, other than our official careers website. Additionally, Farfetch will never ask candidates for any form of payment during the recruitment process.





As part of the People Data & Insights team, you will be a key player in our mission to shape our organizational strategy and empower the business with data-driven insights. We are the analytical and strategic engine that enables the People Team and senior leadership to make smarter, more strategic decisions about our organization and our people.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Want to know how a leading workplace experience and facility management company is powered by technology?

At ISS Tech Team, we create smarter workplace experiences through technology. Our Global Technology Solutions team builds digital solutions, from sensors and IoT devices to data, analytics and AI-driven capabilities, that support our ISS A/S operations across Cleaning, Workplace, Food and Technical Services.

With tech hubs in Copenhagen, Warsaw, and Porto, and teams collaborating across more than 30 countries, our ISS Tech team develops our digital solutions that have a real impact on both our customers and our people.




Location: Porto, Portugal

Work regime: Hybrid







What will you do as Data Analyst:

Provide day‑to‑day operational support for quarterly carbon data collection and input validation.
Maintain strong data‑quality control at the input and pre‑processing stage to ensure smooth downstream reporting by the country decarb managers.
Support the implementation of standardised validation checks, anomaly detection, and country follow‑ups.
Assist in preparing clean, validated datasets for further analysis and reporting.
Contribute to audit‑readiness by organising documentation and evidence.




Key qualifications:

Must have:

Master’s Degree in Data or Environmental Science
More than 3 years’ experience in data handling, environmental data, or similar analytical support roles.
Strong skills in Excel, Power BI (basic visuals, data prep), and SQL (simple queries, data extraction).
Experience with data cleaning, validation, and structured data workflows.
English proficiency, both written and spoken.




Nice have:

Familiarity with emissions data or GHG concepts is an advantage but not required.
Comfortable in repetitive, detail-oriented validation work.




Personal skills:

Strong attention to detail and careful execution.
Strong communication and reliability under time pressure.




What ISS Offers You:

We focus on creating an environment where you can grow and perform at your best:

Permanent contract.
Private health insurance.
22 days of annual leave + 3 additional company days to recharge.
Flexible working hours and a hybrid model that supports balance and collaboration.
Continuous learning opportunities (technical and behavioural development) to support your growth.
Modern, well-equipped office in the heart of Porto.
Free parking for your convenience.
Access to ISS Tech Communities to connect, learn and share knowledge.
Free access to the company gym to support your wellbeing.
Snacks and drinks available at the office.




Why ISS

At ISS, we are more than just a service provider of cleaning, food, workplace and technical services, we are a partner in our customers’ success. By creating exceptional service moments and transforming workplaces into spaces where employees feel valued, engaged and productive, we enhance productivity and help our customers to attract talent and grow their businesses. This begins with our own people through training, career development, and a supportive culture – empowering them to deliver outstanding service. We know that when our people thrive, they create spaces where our customers’ employees and businesses thrive too.




ISS is a Place to Be You.

Be who you are. Become what you want. Be part of something bigger.

Become more. Become ISS.




ISS seeks to BE a place of belonging and CREATE places where every person is welcomed, embraced, and valued for exactly who they are. Places where people feel safe, respected, represented, and supported as their authentic selves.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
🚀 Procuramos um(a) Data Analyst / Data Engineer para integrar uma equipa onde vais fazer a ponte entre negócio, análise de dados e engenharia de dados.




Este papel é ideal para alguém que se sente confortável a falar com stakeholders de negócio, mas também gosta de trabalhar diretamente em pipelines, modelação de dados e soluções técnicas com impacto real.




🧑 💻 Como será o teu dia a dia:

Colaborar com stakeholders para recolher requisitos e traduzi-los em modelos de dados e especificações técnicas
Desenhar, estruturar e manter modelos de dados robustos, como star schemas e snowflake schemas
Desenvolver dashboards e relatórios de alto desempenho para apoiar a decisão de negócio
Garantir data governance, qualidade de dados, consistência e documentação clara
Desenhar, construir, testar e otimizar pipelines ETL/ELT
Trabalhar com ferramentas de ETL, com foco em Stambia, podendo haver adaptação a outras ferramentas do mercado
Implementar automações de qualidade de dados e validações de integridade
Participar ativamente na evolução das soluções de reporting e integração de dados




🧠 O que procuramos:

Formação académica em área relevante, como Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Finance ou similar
Pelo menos 3 anos de experiência em funções como Data Analyst, Analytics Engineer ou BI Developer
Forte componente analítica e experiência prática em desenvolvimento ETL
Excelentes conhecimentos de SQL, incluindo queries complexas, joins, otimização e stored procedures
Bons conhecimentos de modelação de dados e conceitos de data warehouse, com metodologia Kimball
Experiência com ferramentas de ETL, sendo Stambia uma mais-valia forte
Fluência em francês a nível profissional mínimo B2
Fluência em inglês, escrito e falado




⭐ Valorizamos ainda:

Experiência com Power BI, incluindo DAX e Power Query / M
Experiência em banca, seguros ou setor financeiro
Conhecimentos de Azure, AWS ou GCP
Noções de Python ou scripting em Unix/Linux
Capacidade para trabalhar entre equipas técnicas e áreas de negócio




💡 O que oferecemos:

Projeto desafiante com forte componente analítica e técnica
Oportunidade de atuar entre business e data engineering
Ambiente colaborativo e orientado à entrega
Possibilidade de trabalhar com ferramentas e contextos internacionais
Full visa support, se necessário




📍 Localização: Porto, Portugal

⚠️ Modelo: Híbrido




Pacote Salarial Junior: 12.880€ a 21.000€ brutos (14 meses) + Subsídio de Alimentação + Benefícios Flexíveis

Pacote Salarial Mid: 14.000€ a 28.000€ brutos (14 meses) + Subsídio de Alimentação + Benefícios Flexíveis

Pacote Salarial Senior: 20.000€ a 49.000€ brutos (14 meses) + Subsídio de Alimentação + Benefícios Flexíveis

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
In 1966, in Marseille, Pierre Bellon laid the foundations of what would become Sodexo, guided by a simple yet powerful idea: improving everyday life through services that truly matter.

Today, Sodexo is a global leader in Food and Services, supporting people at every moment of life, at work, in schools, hospitals and communities. With 426,000 colleagues across 43 countries, we serve 80 million consumers every day, generating €24.1 billion in revenues. Our growth goes hand in hand with social and environmental responsibility, driven by our purpose: to create a better everyday for everyone and build a better life for all.




About Sodexo Business Services (SBS)




Portugal is no exception! Seven years ago, we established Sodexo Business Services (SBS), our shared service center. Here, you will have the opportunity to join various Financial teams such as Record to Report, Order to Cash, Purchase to Pay, Financial Analysis, and Master Data, supporting European markets and ensuring business growth.




Join Us!

We are looking for talented professionals to be part of our team, bringing unique skills and perspectives to help us grow even further.




Data Analyst

The Data Analyst is responsible for the technical execution of reporting and insight that enhances transparency & business performance with particular emphasis on digitization (Power BI), process efficiency & solution




⚙What will you do?

Enhancing our digital reporting suite & ensuring smooth execution of business-critical activities.
Demonstrate strong technical capability across reporting tools,
Accountable for scoping, designing, and delivering reporting developments.
Explore new ways to leverage reporting technologies, with a focus on building advanced proficiency in tools such as Power BI, Power Automate, and Excel.
Ensure that reporting output and architecture is coherent and consistent with our governance framework and standards.
Evaluate reporting processes and recommend improvements to enhance efficiency and reduce non‑value‑adding analysis.
Engage proactively with stakeholders to deliver reporting and insights, building effective business partnerships and communicating data in a clear, relatable narrative.
Translate broad or unclear stakeholder requirements into structured reporting plans and deliverables.
Be able to shift between data modelling, content creation, commercial insight & business partnering.




🤙What You Need to Succeed:

Bachelor’s degree in relevant field
Fluency in English required, ability to speak other European language is a plus
Focus on produt excellence, integrity and costumer focus
Experiency in Power BI, Power Automate, and Excel.
Continuous improvement mindset
Communication skills
Problem solving
Be flexible and versatile




What we have to offer you?

In 1966, in Marseille, Pierre Bellon laid the foundations of what would become Sodexo, guided by a simple yet powerful idea: improving everyday life through services that truly matter.

Today, Sodexo is a global leader in Food and Services, supporting people at every moment of life, at work, in schools, hospitals and communities. With 426,000 colleagues across 43 countries, we serve 80 million consumers every day, generating €24.1 billion in revenues. Our growth goes hand in hand with social and environmental responsibility, driven by our purpose: to create a better everyday for everyone and build a better life for all.


About Sodexo Business Services (SBS)


Portugal is no exception! Seven years ago, we established Sodexo Business Services (SBS), our shared service center. Here, you will have the opportunity to join various Financial teams such as Record to Report, Order to Cash, Purchase to Pay, Financial Analysis, and Master Data, supporting European markets and ensuring business growth.


Join Us!

We are looking for talented professionals to be part of our team, bringing unique skills and perspectives to help us grow even further.


Data Analyst

The Data Analyst is responsible for the technical execution of reporting and insight that enhances transparency & business performance with particular emphasis on digitization (Power BI), process efficiency & solution


⚙What will you do?

  • Enhancing our digital reporting suite & ensuring smooth execution of business-critical activities.
  • Demonstrate strong technical capability across reporting tools,
  • Accountable for scoping, designing, and delivering reporting developments.
  • Explore new ways to leverage reporting technologies, with a focus on building advanced proficiency in tools such as Power BI, Power Automate, and Excel.
  • Ensure that reporting output and architecture is coherent and consistent with our governance framework and standards.
  • Evaluate reporting processes and recommend improvements to enhance efficiency and reduce non‑value‑adding analysis.
  • Engage proactively with stakeholders to deliver reporting and insights, building effective business partnerships and communicating data in a clear, relatable narrative.
  • Translate broad or unclear stakeholder requirements into structured reporting plans and deliverables.
  • Be able to shift between data modelling, content creation, commercial insight & business partnering.


🤙What You Need to Succeed:

  • Bachelor’s degree in relevant field
  • Fluency in English required, ability to speak other European language is a plus
  • Focus on produt excellence, integrity and costumer focus
  • Experiency in Power BI, Power Automate, and Excel.
  • Continuous improvement mindset
  • Communication skills
  • Problem solving
  • Be flexible and versatile

HTML:
We’re looking for the special, unique and amazing YOU!

@ KCS IT, we look for the ones that stands out, for those that always wants to be better and fight for it, and for those who has the same values that we do: dedication, energy, integrity, transparency, flexibility, trust, honesty, hard work, proactivity, team work.

At KCS we stand for equality and value diversity. We create a safe, diverse environment where opportunities are equal for everyone! We do not discriminate based on age, ethnicity, sexual orientation, gender, disability or any factor other than merit. All applications with skills for the position are welcome!



We are looking for an Amazing: Data Analyst;

Seniority: Mid-Level / Senior (+3 years);

Type of position: Hybrid (Porto);


The amazing you, will have:

• Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics or related fields;

• Professional experience in Data Analysis;

• Strong experience with Python (Pandas);

• Experience with SQL and data manipulation;

• Professional experience with Tableau and/or Power BI;

• Experience with Power Query and DAX;

• Knowledge of Big Data technologies such as PySpark, Presto or Airflow;

• Experience creating dashboards, reports and data visualizations;

• Strong problem-solving and analytical skills;

• Experience gathering business requirements and translating them into technical solutions;

• Ability to communicate with both technical and non-technical stakeholders;

• Fluent level of English;


Who are we?

Founded in 2008 and based in Lisbon, KCS IT is a consulting company in the field of Information Technology and Services, focused on creating value for our clients through three main areas: Consulting, Outsourcing, Innovation and Training. Our commitment to talent development is unmistakable in the recent opening of the Porto, Leiria and Azores hubs, which aims to develop technology for the national and international market. Since 2018 we have been elected one of the 10 major companies in the “Index of Excellence”, an initiative that aims to reward organizations that invest most in the development and satisfaction of their employees.

Title: Data Analyst – Dataiku
Description:
🌍 About Keyrus

Keyrus is a global consulting and technology company focused on architecting intelligence, helping organisations transform data, digital capabilities, and AI into sustainable business value.

Founded in 1996, Keyrus operates in 28+ countries across 5 continents, with more than 3,300 people worldwide. Our strength comes from combining deep expertise in Data & Analytics, AI, Digital, and Management Consulting with a strong understanding of business realities.

Data is never the goal in itself.

We use data to shape understanding, design meaningful experiences, and enable better, real-life decisions.

At Keyrus, we also believe companies have a responsibility beyond performance. Through our Foundation and ESG initiatives, we actively contribute to sustainability, inclusion, and positive societal impact.

#OneTeamOneKeyrus

🎯 The Role

As a Data Analyst, you will work at the intersection of data, technology, and business, helping transform complex datasets into actionable insights that support business, compliance, and operational decision-making.

You will collaborate with multidisciplinary teams in an international environment and contribute to data-driven initiatives that improve reporting, controls, and business performance.

📍 Job location: Porto or Lisbon (Hybrid)

🕒 Contract type: Employee

🗓 Target start date: ASAP

⏰ Working hours: Full-time (40h/week)

💵 Compensation: €35,000 – €45,000/year

Note: All applications (CVs) must be submitted in English.

💥 Your Impact

In This Role, You Will


Gather and analyse business requirements from stakeholders.
Translate business needs into data workflows and reporting solutions.
Develop Dataiku workflows and reconciliation processes.
Create impactful Power BI dashboards and visualizations.
Monitor data quality and perform validation activities.
Generate actionable insights to support decision-making.
Support compliance, controls, and operational reporting initiatives.


⚡ What Makes This Role Challenging


You will work with complex and highly regulated datasets.
Business requirements often evolve and require adaptable solutions.
Data quality and reporting accuracy are critical.
You will collaborate with both business and technical stakeholders.


👤 What We’re Looking For

Must-haves


Bachelor's or Master's Degree in a relevant field.
3+ years of experience in Data Analysis, Business Intelligence, or Analytics.
Dataiku DSS experience.
Strong Power BI skills.
Advanced SQL knowledge.
Experience with data validation, reconciliation, and reporting.
Strong analytical and communication skills.
Fluency in English.
Must be a Portuguese or European citizen, or hold a valid work permit for Portugal.


Nice-to-haves


Python.
Banking or Financial Services experience.
Data Governance exposure.
Regulatory reporting knowledge.
French language skills.
Alteryx or similar workflow automation tools.


💰 How Our Salary Ranges Work

At Keyrus, salary ranges reflect different levels of mastery and impact within the same role — not different job titles.


Bottom of the range You meet the core requirements and will need ramp-up time and support.
Middle of the range You are fully autonomous from Day 1 and deliver consistently.
Top of the range You are a reference for the role, mentor others, and raise the bar for the team.


Final offers are based on experience, autonomy, scope, and market context, and are discussed transparently during the process.

🎁 What We Offer At Keyrus Portugal


Competitive salary aligned with your experience and the data market
Meal allowance: €10.20/day
Flexible benefits plan
Private medical insurance
22 days of annual leave, increasing every 3 years (up to 25 days)
Continuous learning via KLX – Keyrus Learning Experience
A collaborative, international, and human-centred work environment


💙 Why Keyrus?

Joining Keyrus Means Joining


A market leader in Data Intelligence
A company where people, trust, and diversity are core values
An environment that values ownership, flexibility, and innovation
A place where different backgrounds and perspectives are not just welcomed = they are essential


We believe diversity drives better thinking, stronger teams, and better outcomes.

Everyone belongs at Keyrus.

At Keyrus, all stages of our recruitment process are conducted and evaluated by human recruiters and interviewers.

To support accuracy and efficiency, AI may occasionally be used internally by our team exclusively for note-taking purposes during interviews. AI is never used to make hiring decisions or evaluate candidates.

🔒 To ensure fairness, authenticity, and the protection of confidential and proprietary information, the use of AI tools by candidates during the recruitment process is strictly prohibited.

Our commitment to responsible AI practices ensures that hiring decisions are based solely on each candidate’s own skills, experience, judgment, and expertise.

⚠️ Any use of AI assistance during the interview process may result in immediate disqualification from the recruitment process.

♿ Equal Opportunity Statement

We are committed to building an inclusive workplace and encourage applications from all backgrounds, regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

Offering both public and private charging points, EVIO ensures accessibility via an easy-to-use global app.

The platform enables users to share and monetize their chargers in locations such as companies, condominiums, and private garages, complemented by power management and other digital services.

EVIO is committed to making electric vehicle charging seamless, efficient, and accessible.

This profile should be able to work with relational and non relational databases, data warehouses, data lakes, Python, and Power BI, creating a solid foundation for reporting, analytics, and decision making.

The role also involves collaborating with cross-functional teams to deploy machine learning models and optimize data processes for improved decision-making.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are growing. And we are looking for talented people.




At Decskill, we believe that technological excellence is driven by human talent.

We are an IT consulting company with more than 10 years of consolidated experience in the market, focused on building long-term relationships with both our clients and our people. Today, we are a community of over 800 professionals, working from Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid, contributing to impactful technology initiatives.




As part of the Astek Group, we combine a strong local culture with a global presence, being active in around 23 countries across 4 continents. This allows us to offer an international context, diverse challenges, and long-term career opportunities, while staying close to our teams.




We are looking for an Data Engineer!




Responsibilities:

Perform analysis of internal and external data to identify business growth opportunities and support client strategy initiatives.
Ensure accurate tracking and delivery of KPIs aligned with client development goals and business roadmap.
Support global teams (London, New York, Hong Kong) by providing high-quality data insights, tools, and analysis to improve sales effectiveness and decision-making.
Produce high-value reports, dashboards, and documentation for regional and global client reviews.
Leverage multiple data sources to generate actionable insights on business performance, client profitability, and wallet share growth.
Promote and contribute to the automation of reporting and client intelligence deliverables in collaboration with business and IT teams.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across multiple data pipelines, dashboards, and reporting systems.
Support the development and improvement of client data processes, analytics frameworks, and reporting standards.




Requirements:

Technical Requirements

Strong experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or data engineering-related roles.
Solid knowledge of:
Data visualization and BI tools such as:
Tableau
Power BI
Strong programming skills in Python, particularly using Pandas for data analysis.
Strong knowledge of SQL and experience working with ETL processes.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel.
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing.
Experience working with large, complex datasets and multi-source data environments.
Experience in report automation and data pipeline optimization is highly valued.

Soft Skills

Strong communication and presentation skills, with the ability to translate data into clear business insights.
Adaptability and ability to work in fast-paced, global environments.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data quality and accuracy.
Results-driven mindset with strong delivery focus.
Problem-solving skills with the ability to design and improve analytical processes.
Proactive attitude and ability to work effectively within a team.
Strong collaboration skills across business and technical stakeholders.

Language Requirements

English: Mastery level (mandatory).




Are you looking for an environment that values curiosity and commitment? Here, your contribution has real impact and individual growth is taken seriously.




Find with us the right opportunity to grow!




What you can expect from us:

Long-term projects with national and international context (if applicable)
Opportunities to grow technically and professionally
A people-first culture, focused on transparency and trust
Teams that value ownership, collaboration and stability.




If you’re interested in this job please send your CV to cesaltina.abreu@decskill.com, with the reference “CA/DataAnalyst”.




Thank you! :)




Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being.

Title: Analista de Dados (M/F)
HTML:
A GreenVenture é uma empresa privada de investimento e gestão de ativos especializada na identificação, aquisição, desenvolvimento e implementação de projetos de energia renovável.


Acreditamos no poder da ação coletiva e da inovação para criar um futuro sustentável. Procuramos reforçar a nossa estrutura com um Analista de dados (m/f).


Missão:

Para suportar o crescimento das nossas áreas de produção e comercialização de energia, procuramos profissional para desenvolvimento de modelos matemáticos aplicados a mercados de eletricidade;


Responsabilidades:

  • Desenvolvimento e manutenção de modelos de previsão multi-variável e multi-horizonte (produção solar e eólica, consumo, preço, desvios);
  • Desenvolver dashboards de monitorização de desempenho e relatórios analíticos (KPIs de precisão, eficiência e retorno);
  • Implementar rotinas de data cleaning, atualização automática de datasets e version control de modelos;
  • Sistemas de decisão em tempo real para mercados grossistas e mercados de serviços de sistema;
  • Colaborar com a equipa técnica e de gestão na interpretação dos resultados preditivos e na sua integração nas decisões de trading;
  • Validação, monitorização e backtesting de modelos em ambiente operacional;
  • Colaboração com a equipa de operação na definição de requisitos e avaliação de resultados.


Perfil:

  • Formação superior em Matemática (pura ou aplicada), Física ou Engenharia Física. Áreas afins com sólida base quantitativa (Estatística, Data Science) poderão ser consideradas;
  • Domínio sólido de Python (numpy, pandas); familiaridade com bibliotecas de ML e otimização (scikit-learn, PyTorch, Pyomo, OR-Tools) constitui uma mais-valia;
  • Conforto com séries temporais, modelação probabilística, otimização sob restrições e teoria da decisão;
  • Capacidade de trabalho com dados incompletos, multi-fonte e em diferentes granularidades temporais;
  • Pensamento crítico e rigor analítico.


Oferta:

  • Integração numa empresa sólida e em crescimento;
  • Equipa com estrutura profissional e dinâmica;
  • Remuneração em função da experiência demonstrada.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Unleash the Power of Data & AI — Drive Business Transformation with Cutting-Edge BI and Data Science




Porto-based opportunity with hybrid work model (up to 3 days remote per week).

As a Mid-Level Power BI and Dataiku Data Engineer, you will be working for our client, a leader in data-driven innovation and AI integration. You will help transform complex data into actionable insights, supporting advanced analytics and Generative AI initiatives that propel the business forward. This role offers a unique opportunity to shape impactful data solutions and advance your career in a dynamic industry.




Your main responsibilities:

Design, develop, and maintain interactive Power BI dashboards and reports tailored to business requirements.
Build efficient data models in Power BI, adhering to best practices for measures, KPIs, and data storytelling.
Perform data preparation and feature engineering using Dataiku’s visual recipes and code-based workflows.
Collaborate with Data Engineers to ensure datasets are clean, reliable, and analytics-ready.
Contribute to data science workflows within Dataiku, including exploratory data analysis and model testing.
Support Generative AI use cases, such as prompt-based analytics, LLM integrations, and AI-assisted insights.
Ensure data quality, consistency, and usability across reporting and analytical assets.
Participate in documentation, knowledge sharing, and adherence to best practices within the team.

Proven experience working with Dataiku for data preparation and analytics workflows.
Strong understanding of data visualization principles and storytelling.

Title: Data Analyst – Commercial (m/f/d)
HTML:
Chemical Industry | Data Analyst – Commercial



Your new Company


Our client is a Global Recognized Organization operating at the forefront of advanced materials and specialty solutions. With a strong international footprint and a workforce of around 1 000 professionals.


Driven by deep scientific expertise and a strong commitment to innovation, the Organization leverages advanced capabilities in material sciences, formulation technologies, and surface modification to deliver tailored solutions.


With a clear focus on innovation, operational excellence, and professional development, this is an opportunity to join a Company that combines global scale with a dynamic and forward-thinking mindset.



Your new Role


The Data Analyst – Commercial transforms complex Datasets into clear, actionable insights that support decision making across the organization. Working within a Matrix structure and closely aligned with the Commercial function, this person partners with business stakeholders to understand priorities, interpret commercial Data, and deliver high-quality analysis, reporting, dashboards, and presentations.


This person reports to the Business Data Manager, and will collaborate closely with Commercial stakeholders, Business Leaders, Technical Specialists, and Data Governance teams to support performance cycles, improve reporting capabilities, and strengthen data-driven decision making across the business.


The Data Analyst – Commercial will:

  • Partner with Commercial stakeholders to understand business priorities and deliver Data-driven insights that support operational and strategic decision-making;
  • Produce accurate and timely management reporting, analyzing commercial performance and identifying trends, risks, opportunities, and deviations from plan;
  • Support functional performance cycles (including sales pipeline reviews, demand planning, SKU rationalization activities, and sales forecasting);
  • Execute and support Data creation, updates, clean-up, archiving, and deletion activities related to customer Data, pricing, rebates, discounts, commissions, sales areas, and commercial taxonomies;
  • Ensure commercial Data is accurate and consistent across business systems, validating and preparing Datasets in line with Data governance standards;
  • Act as a subject matter expert in SalesForce, providing user support, training, guidance, and continuous improvement recommendations;
  • Develop, maintain, and enhance Power BI dashboards, reports, and visualizations to improve visibility of commercial performance and decision-making;
  • Prepare and deliver reports, presentations, and commercial insights for sales meetings, customer reviews, and other business reviews;
  • Prepare reports linking SalesForce and Sales Data and support updates of R&D/NBO pipeline information for internal and external communications;
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to improve Data quality, analytical capabilities, reporting processes, and business tools;


A typical day will include analyzing commercial performance, maintaining dashboards, supporting sales and demand planning activities, attending sales meetings, validating Data quality, supporting SalesForce users, and delivering insights that support key business decisions.


Please consider that this role requires availability for occasional travel to other company sites, approximately once per year.



What you will need to succeed


As a Data Analyst – Commercial, you will need:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Information Systems, Data Management, or another analytical or business-related field;
  • +3 years of experience within an International Manufacturing or Industrial environment;
  • +3 years of experience in analytical or support roles within Sales, Commercial, or related business functions;
  • Proven experience developing and maintaining Power BI dashboards, reports, and Data visualizations that deliver actionable business insights;
  • Experience supporting demand planning, sales performance reviews, pipeline analysis, customer Data management, pricing, and commercial reporting;
  • Advanced Data collection, cleansing, validation, and reporting skills;
  • Experience working within Matrix Organizations and collaborating with Cross-Functional Teams (would be a plus);
  • Advanced Excel skills (including Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP, advanced functions, and graphs);
  • Experience with project management tools;
  • Knowledge of Python, SQL, and SharePoint management (would be a plus);
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver high-quality outputs in a fast-paced international environment;
  • Excellent communication skills – Fluency in English.


What the Company can offer you


Have the opportunity to join a cross-functional team in an international company with a multicultural working environment!



Next Steps


If you are interested in this opportunity, please send us your updated CV. If you are looking for another type of professional challenge, please contact us to discuss other career opportunities, always in complete confidence.

Title: Sustainability Data Analyst
Description:
Want to know how a leading workplace experience and facility management company is powered by technology?

At ISS Tech Team, we create smarter workplace experiences through technology. Our Global Technology Solutions team builds digital solutions, from sensors and IoT devices to data, analytics and AI-driven capabilities, that support our ISS A/S operations across Cleaning, Workplace, Food and Technical Services.

With tech hubs in Copenhagen, Warsaw, and Porto, and teams collaborating across more than 30 countries, our ISS Tech team develops our digital solutions that have a real impact on both our customers and our people.

Location: Porto, Portugal

Work regime: Hybrid

What Will You Do As Data Analyst


Provide day‑to‑day operational support for quarterly carbon data collection and input validation.
Maintain strong data‑quality control at the input and pre‑processing stage to ensure smooth downstream reporting by the country decarb managers.
Support the implementation of standardised validation checks, anomaly detection, and country follow‑ups.
Assist in preparing clean, validated datasets for further analysis and reporting.
Contribute to audit‑readiness by organising documentation and evidence.


Key Qualifications

Must have:


Master’s Degree in Data or Environmental Science
More than 3 years’ experience in data handling, environmental data, or similar analytical support roles.
Strong skills in Excel, Power BI (basic visuals, data prep), and SQL (simple queries, data extraction).
Experience with data cleaning, validation, and structured data workflows.
English proficiency, both written and spoken.


Nice Have


Familiarity with emissions data or GHG concepts is an advantage but not required.
Comfortable in repetitive, detail-oriented validation work.


Personal Skills


Strong attention to detail and careful execution.
Strong communication and reliability under time pressure.


What ISS Offers You

We focus on creating an environment where you can grow and perform at your best:


Permanent contract.
Private health insurance.
22 days of annual leave + 3 additional company days to recharge.
Flexible working hours and a hybrid model that supports balance and collaboration.
Continuous learning opportunities (technical and behavioural development) to support your growth.
Modern, well-equipped office in the heart of Porto.
Free parking for your convenience.
Access to ISS Tech Communities to connect, learn and share knowledge.
Free access to the company gym to support your wellbeing.
Snacks and drinks available at the office.


Why ISS

At ISS, we are more than just a service provider of cleaning, food, workplace and technical services, we are a partner in our customers’ success. By creating exceptional service moments and transforming workplaces into spaces where employees feel valued, engaged and productive, we enhance productivity and help our customers to attract talent and grow their businesses. This begins with our own people through training, career development, and a supportive culture – empowering them to deliver outstanding service. We know that when our people thrive, they create spaces where our customers’ employees and businesses thrive too.

ISS is a Place to Be You.

Be who you are. Become what you want. Be part of something bigger.

Become more. Become ISS.

ISS seeks to BE a place of belonging and CREATE places where every person is welcomed, embraced, and valued for exactly who they are. Places where people feel safe, respected, represented, and supported as their authentic selves.

Title: IT Expert Data, Analytics & Risk
HTML:
Queres integrar uma das maiores organizações na prestação de serviços profissionais do mercado?

Então a Deloitte é para ti. Aqui, vais ter a possibilidade de potenciar a tua carreira e de reforçar as tuas competências no nosso universo tecnológico.

Somos um Tech Hub internacional e estamos à procura dos melhores especialistas em IT Risk para se juntarem aos nossos centros de excelência e à nossa comunidade. Na Deloitte, vais transformar o mundo como o conhecemos e desenvolver soluções únicas e inovadoras nos mais variados e complexos projetos de transformação, de Portugal para o mundo. Vamos reforçar a nossa área de Strategy, Risk and Transaction e procuramos profissionais experientes que tenham gosto e aptidão natural para trabalhar em equipa e em ambiente multi-projeto. Podes descobrir todas as funções que vais desempenhar, de acordo com o teu perfil, para assim dares um salto na tua carreira!

Juntas-te a nós? Estamos à tua espera.

O teu dia a dia e responsabilidades:

  • Analisar grandes volumes de dados provenientes de diferentes sistemas e tecnologias para tratamento, identificação de padrões, tendências e indicadores de risco;
  • Desenvolver modelos quantitativos e qualitativos para avaliação e previsão de riscos, utilizando técnicas estatísticas e ferramentas analíticas;
  • Implementar processos de data governance para garantir a qualidade, integridade e segurança dos dados utilizados na análise de riscos;
  • Gerir o risco tecnológico dentro da organização, desenvolvendo e implementando estratégias de mitigação de riscos;
  • Assegurar a segurança da informação, incluindo a proteção de dados confidenciais e a prevenção de incidentes de segurança cibernética;
  • Realizar auditorias informáticas para avaliar a conformidade com os padrões e políticas de segurança estabelecidos;
  • Desenvolver e implementar planos de continuidade de negócios para garantir a resiliência operacional em caso de interrupções ou desastres;
  • Desenvolver soluções eficazes para garantir a resiliência operacional em face de interrupções ou desastres.


És tu quem procuramos?

  • Licenciatura e/ou Mestrado em Engenharia Informática, Engenharia Eletrotécnica, Informática de Gestão ou áreas afins;
  • Certificações relevantes em segurança da informação, auditoria informática ou continuidade de negócios são desejáveis;
  • Conhecimentos sólidos em análise de dados e capacidade de trabalhar com grandes volumes de dados com integração de fontes internas e externas;
  • Mínimo de 1 a 6 anos em funções relacionadas a auditoria IT, segurança da informação ou gestão de riscos;
  • Experiência com ferramentas e linguagens analíticas e de processamento de dados (SAS, Python, SQL, entre outros);
  • Experiência com ferramentas e tecnologias de segurança da informação, como SIEM, IAM, DLP, entre outros;
  • Familiaridade com padrões e regulamentos de segurança, como ISO 27001, GDPR, NIST, entre outros.


Desenvolve uma carreira à tua medida

  • Num contexto de trabalho diverso que permite um desenvolvimento e aprendizagem contínuos;
  • Através de um plano de desenvolvimento de carreira personalizado (skills/ oportunidades técnicas e funcionais), adequado ao momento, às tuas necessidades e objetivos;
  • Participando em projetos nacionais e internacionais, com dimensão e impacto relevante nesta área de especialização;
  • Acesso ilimitado às maiores plataformas de aprendizagem online (Linkedin Learning e Udemy);
  • Oportunidade de formações e certificações nas mais recentes tecnologias, nomeadamente em tecnologias Cloud.


Vive uma cultura de união

  • Uma cultura de inovação e excelência que nos torna líderes e precursores de tendências;
  • Um ambiente amigável, de respeito, confiança, transparência e informalidade;
  • Uma rede para a vida que te faz estar ligado ao melhor conhecimento ao lado de líderes e especialistas;
  • Uma oportunidade para fazeres parte das nossas comunidades (de Tech, inovação, desporto e outros interesses) e para participares ativamente em projetos de responsabilidade social.


Sobre a Deloitte

Na Deloitte nunca trabalharás sozinho. Terás a oportunidade de integrar diversos projetos de transformação e fazer parte de uma equipa que te vai inspirar e ajudar a desenvolver soluções diferenciadoras, num ambiente dinâmico e colaborativo, onde podes dar o melhor de ti e beneficiar das nossas iniciativas de bem-estar e flexibilidade. Somos mais de 5.500 pessoas em Portugal, das quais mais de 2.600 trabalham em áreas tecnológicas, a partir de escritórios e Digital Studios em Lisboa, Porto, Braga, Viseu, Coimbra e Faro e de polos tecnológicos em Braga. Além disso, as nossas equipas em Portugal têm projeção internacional, o que demonstra a nossa forte liderança global em low code/no code, em engenharia de redes de telecomunicações, em plataformas tecnológicas de transformação organizacional (ServiceNow e Apptio) e na indústria de serviços financeiros (Finastra, TIA e Guidewire).

Se tens interesse em explorar e desenvolver as tuas competências nalguma destas áreas em soluções tecnológicas – engenharia de software, cloud, integração de sistemas, data analytics & data science, cyber risk, engenharia de redes telecomunicações, UX design - e gostarias de trabalhar com tecnologias como AWS, Google, Oracle, Salesforce, SAP, Mulesoft, Feedzai e SAS, entre outras, então estás no lugar certo!

Sabe mais em www.deloitte.com/pt/careers .

Keywords: Sobre a Deloitte; Consulting; Technology

Title: Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst
Description:
HBK is a leading organization in the testing and measurement industry that is on a journey to ensure it leverages data-driven insights to make strategic business decisions. We are seeking a highly motivated and skilled Business Intelligence Analyst to join our dynamic team. The successful candidate will play a pivotal role in enhancing our data analytics and reporting capabilities. The team is currently rebuilding our data landscape, and this is a real opportunity to drive change within the business area.

Job Summary:

As a Business Intelligence Analyst, you will play a key role in gathering, analyzing, and transforming data into actionable insights that drive informed decision-making at HBK. Drawing upon your extensive expertise in Business Intelligence, you will collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver advanced data solutions and enhance operational efficiency.

Key Responsibilities:


Data Strategy: Develop and execute a comprehensive data strategy that leverages our data assets to meet the organization’s analytical needs and objectives.
Advanced Analytics: Apply advanced statistical and predictive analytics techniques to uncover trends, patterns, and insights in data, enabling more informed decision-making.
Reporting Excellence: Lead the design and maintenance of sophisticated, interactive reports and dashboards using modern business intelligence and data visualization platforms (SAP Analytics Cloud, Power BI, or similar tools). Create highly visual and intuitive data visualizations for use by business stakeholders.
Data Integration and Governance: Oversee the integration of data from diverse sources, ensuring data quality, consistency, and adherence to data governance policies. Collaborate with IT and data engineering teams to optimize data pipelines.
Performance Optimization: Continuously enhance the performance of our systems, including optimizing data processing and reporting capabilities.
Business Requirements Analysis: Work closely with business leaders to understand their analytical needs and translate these requirements into actionable data solutions.
Data Modeling Expertise: Design and implement complex data models to facilitate advanced reporting and analytics, encompassing both logical and physical data models.
Data Security and Compliance: Ensure strict data security and compliance with internal policies and industry regulations throughout all data handling and reporting activities.
Mentorship and Training: Provide guidance and training to other analysts and end-users, fostering self-service analytics adoption.


Qualifications:


Bachelor's degree in Business, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field (Master's degree preferred).
A minimum of 3 years of experience as a Business Intelligence Analyst.
In-depth expertise in data warehousing, ETL processes, data modeling, and advanced analytics techniques.
Proficiency in SQL and familiarity with data visualization tools.
Exceptional problem-solving skills and analytical thinking.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Proven ability to work effectively within cross-functional teams.
Knowledge of industry best practices and emerging trends in business intelligence and data analytics.


Additional Information:


Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits package.
Opportunities for career growth and professional development within our organization.
A collaborative and innovative work environment equipped with state-of-the-art data analytics tools.


If you are a seasoned professional with a passion for driving advanced data-driven decision-making, we invite you to apply and become a pivotal part of our team at HBK. Join us in shaping the future of data analytics for our business's success.

We Offer

Freedom with responsibility is the framework for HBK’s employees. This allows for a good balance between work and family life and for constant development of professional and personal skills in an international and enjoyable working environment.

One Company – HBK

On 1 January 2019, Brüel & Kjær and HBM merged their activities into a new company called HBK (Hottinger Brüel & Kjær). HBK is a subsidiary of Spectris Limited, and employees 3,500 employees worldwide and production facilities in Denmark, Germany, the UK, the USA, China and Portugal and presence in 80 countries, HBK is a leading player in the test and measurement area. For further info please visit www.hbkworld.com.

Title: Data Steward
HTML:
A Job? Or a Lifetime Experience? Start Yours Here!


A nossa missão é ser uma parte significativa da carreira das nossas pessoas. À medida que crescemos, cresce também a determinação em oferecer a melhor experiência aos nossos colaboradores e clientes — e é exatamente isso que nos move.

Somos uma empresa portuguesa de consultoria tecnológica com escritórios em Lisboa, Porto e Óbidos e representações no Brasil e Tunísia. Contamos com mais de 12 anos de expertise no mercado e hoje somos um universo de cerca de 400 pessoas a trabalhar em contexto presencial, remoto ou híbrido, em projetos em mais de 20 países.

Acreditamos que grandes pessoas fazem empresas de sucesso. Por isso, defendemos a valorização, o reconhecimento e o crescimento contínuo dos nossos profissionais.

Não queremos ser apenas mais uma linha no teu CV. Queremos que vivas connosco uma verdadeira Lifetime Experience. Apostamos em formação e certificações que comprovam a tua progressão real, promovemos um saudável work-life balance e oferecemos benefícios com impacto direto na tua vida pessoal e carreira.

#welcometoyourfuture


O que procuramos:

  • +3 anos de experiência em Data Management, Business Analysis ou funções similares;
  • Bons conhecimentos de Data Governance, incluindo qualidade de dados e gestão de metadados;
  • Familiaridade com ferramentas de Data Governance (Data Catalogs, Data Quality Observability);
  • Capacidade de comunicação e colaboração entre equipas técnicas e de negócio;
  • Pensamento crítico, atenção ao detalhe e foco na resolução de problemas;
  • Conhecimentos em Python, JavaScript ou linguagens similares para scripting;
  • Inglês fluente.


Ser Affinity


· #Affinidade – Criares relações de proximidade e empatia com colegas, clientes e candidatos (e não apenas com tecnologias, linguagens e plataformas). Espírito de equipa e boa disposição;

· #Ambição – Querer fazer mais e melhor. Vontade de exceder expectativas e evoluir pessoal e profissionalmente;

· #Ação – Energia para fazer acontecer. Proatividade e capacidade de iniciativa;

· #Aprendizagem – Vontade de evoluir individual e coletivamente e de te tornares uma referência no mercado tecnológico;

· #Assertividade – Comunicação transparente e honesta, sempre com feedback construtivo.

A tua #LifetimeExperience

Title: Business Insights Analyst
Description:
This is a remote role.

About Us

Reachdesk is the first truly end-to-end global SaaS gifting and swag platform, helping B2B companies create meaningful connections with prospects, customers, and employees: while driving measurable ROI.

We believe gifting is more than just a transaction: it’s about building genuine human connections that fuel business growth. Whether you’re engaging prospects, celebrating customers, or recognizing employees, our platform makes it easy to deliver personalized gifts, branded swag, and unforgettable event & brand experiences at scale through automated sourcing, storage, and global delivery.

Trusted by many of the most recognized names in technology and SaaS, Reachdesk integrates effortlessly with your marketing, sales, and HRIS tech stack and is powered by a world-class team with hubs in New York, London, and Lisbon.

We’re growing, and we’re currently looking for a Business Insights Analyst to join our Business Systems & Operations team.

The Role

This is a role for someone energised by the connections between how the business sells, how it serves customers, how it runs operationally and makes money, and who wants to use AI to make those connections faster and clearer than ever.

Every function at Reachdesk sees a different slice of the company. You'll be the person who sits across all of them, using available data and reporting outputs from relevant systems, helping leaders apply consistent commercial and operational definitions when interpreting business performance, and helping leaders see the picture none of them could have produced alone.

Your Responsibilities


Partner with leaders across Sales, Customer Success, Marketing, Finance and Operations to understand the decisions they're each trying to make.
Work with available data from commercial, finance and operational systems to produce insights that connect different views of the business.
Use AI to compress synthesis from days into hours, and to keep our framing of key metrics consistent across functions.
Tell the story behind the numbers in a way that lands with whoever's in the room, commercial, financial or operational.
Create lightweight summaries and repeatable insight outputs so teams can keep answering their own follow-on questions.
Experiment with AI-assisted insight workflows, prompt patterns, evals and lightweight automations, and share what works across the team.


About You


2–4 years in an analyst, insights, strategy or consulting role with exposure to more than one function.
Fluency in at least two of: commercial (revenue, retention, GTM), financial (P&L, unit economics, FP&A), operational (throughput, fulfilment, cycle time).
A genuine AI-native working style; you already use LLMs as a core part of how you think, and you're excited to keep pushing.
Strong written and verbal storytelling; you can adapt your framing for a CRO, a CFO or a Head of Operations without missing a beat.
Comfort working with spreadsheets and reporting tools. Working knowledge of SQL is useful but not essential.


Preferred Expertise


B2B SaaS background.
Familiarity with NetSuite, Salesforce, or the corporate gifting and direct mail space.
Light experience with experiment design, attribution, pricing or operational throughput modeling.


Why Join Us


A team that genuinely treats AI as a leverage tool, not a buzzword.
The closest thing in the company to a true cross-functional vantage point; you'll see how every part of Reachdesk fits together.
You’ll work alongside a small, senior Business Systems & Operations team focused on strategic, cross-functional work.


The Benefits


Competitive salary with generous stock options
Flexible PTO that increases the longer you’re with us, plus extra flexibility for life’s unexpected moments
A genuinely collaborative culture with innovative, fun, and ambitious people


Privacy Policy: We process personal information submitted as part of your application in accordance with the Reachdesk Candidate Privacy Notice. Please review Reachdesk’s Privacy Policy here: www.reachdesk.com/candidate-privacy-policy

Title: Data Analytics Expert (m/f/d)
Description:
Linde Portugal, LDA

Data Analytics Expert (m/f/d)

Porto or Lisbon, Portugal | req29166

What You Will Enjoy Doing


As a Data Analytics expert, you will act as a key speaking partner for a regional business team to discuss and understand local business requirements and translating them into data products
You will design, develop, and maintain a portfolio of automated, high quality data products for the region to support local reporting needs
You will discuss and support regional ad-hoc data requests and provide data insights in a timely and qualitative manner using your technical as well as business expertise
You will work closely together with the data engineering team to enhance and ensure robust data pipelines to allow scalable and automated deliverables
You will collaborate/ align regional demands with the data science and data analytics teams to ensure synergies, best practices and scalability.


What Makes You Great


You possess a minimum of a masters degree from a university in a STEM subject (science technology, engineering or mathematics)
You possess a strong business understanding and be able to transfer business requirements into data pipelines, data models and data insights
You have expert knowledge in programming with SQL, R, Python and a track record in building customer facing applications and reports using Power BI and web application frameworks like R shiny or similar
You have very good communication skills ideally combined with experience in change management
You combine interest and motivation to continuously learn and develop with excellent spoken and written skills in English
Willingness to travel to collaborate with the wider team and business (

Why you will love working with us

At Linde you will be surrounded by knowledgeable people who love what they do. They are creative and strive to move our company forward. When facing adverse conditions they work out unique technical concepts, in order to find the best solutions for our customers. At the same time we are aware of our responsibility towards the safety of our employees at their workplace.

What we offer you!

At Linde, the sky is not the limit. If you’re looking to build a career where your work reaches beyond your job description and betters the people with whom you work, the communities we serve, and the world in which we all live, at Linde, your opportunities are limitless. Be Linde. Be Limitless.

In addition to an attractive compensation package, we offer you many social benefits, such as an excellent pension plan, flexible working hours and a wide range of health care options. Furthermore, we support you with various offers to help you combine family and career and to support your professional and personal development.

Have we inspired you? Let´s talk about it

We are looking forward to receiving your complete application (motivation letter, CV, certificates) via our online job market.

Any designations used of course apply to persons of all genders. The form of speech used here is for simplicity only.

Linde Portugal, LDA acts responsibly towards its shareholders, business partners, employees, society and the environment in every one of its business areas, regions and locations across the globe. The company is committed to technologies and products that unite the goals of customer value and sustainable development.
